QualityCheckResult( check_name=f"unique_{'_'.join(columns)}", table_name=table_name, passed=passed, total_rows=total_rows, failed_rows=duplicate_rows, failure_percentage=failure_pct, ) def check_referential_integrity( df: DataFrame, ref_df: DataFrame, column: str, ref_column: str, table_name: str, ref_table_name: str, ) -> QualityCheckResult: """Check referential integrity between tables. Args: df: Source DataFrame ref_df: Reference DataFrame column: Column in source table ref_column: Column in reference table table_name: Name of source table ref_table_name: Name of reference table Returns: Check result """ total_rows = df.count()

    # Get orphaned rows (source values not in reference)
    orphaned = df.join(
        ref_df.select(col(ref_column).alias("_ref_key")),
        df[column] == col("_ref_key"),
        "left_anti",
    )
    orphaned_count = orphaned.count()

    passed = orphaned_count == 0
    failure_pct = (orphaned_count / total_rows * 100) if total_rows > 0 else 0

    return QualityCheckResult(

        table_name=table_name,
        passed=passed,

        failed_rows=orphaned_count,
        failure_percentage=failure_pct,
        details=f"References {ref_table_name}.{ref_column}",
    )


def run_quality_checks(
    spark: SparkSession, table_name: str, checks: list[dict]
) -> list[QualityCheckResult]:
    """Run a set of quality checks on a table.

    Args:
        spark: Spark session
        table_name: Fully qualified table name
        checks: List of check configurations

    Returns:
        List of check results
    """
    logger.info(f"Running quality checks on {table_name}")
    df = spark.read.format("delta").table(table_name)
    results: list[QualityCheckResult] = []

    for check in checks:
        check_type = check["type"]

        if check_type == "not_null":
            results.extend(check_not_null(df, check["columns"], table_name))
        elif check_type == "unique":
            results.append(check_unique(df, check["columns"], table_name))
        elif check_type == "referential_integrity":
            ref_df = spark.read.format("delta").table(check["ref_table"])
            results.append(
                check_referential_integrity(
                    df,
                    ref_df,
                    check["column"],
                    check["ref_column"],
                    table_name,
                    check["ref_table"],
                )
            )

    passed = sum(1 for r in results if r.passed)
    total = len(results)
    logger.info(f"Quality checks complete: {passed}/{total} passed")

    return results

"""Silver layer transformation functions."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable

from pyspark.sql import DataFrame
from pyspark.sql.functions import (
    col,
    current_timestamp,
    from_json,
    get_json_object,
    lit,
    to_date,
    to_timestamp,
    when,
)
from pyspark.sql.types import StringType, StructField, StructType

from workbench.config.logging import get_logger
from workbench.config.settings import get_settings

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

logger = get_logger(__name__)


# Transformation registry for different table types
TRANSFORM_REGISTRY: dict[str, Callable[[DataFrame], DataFrame]] = {}


def register_transform(table_name: str) -> Callable:
    """Decorator to register a transformation function."""

    def decorator(func: Callable[[DataFrame], DataFrame]) -> Callable:
        TRANSFORM_REGISTRY[table_name] = func
        return func

    return decorator


@register_transform("programs")
def transform_programs(df: DataFrame) -> DataFrame:
    """Transform programs from Notion."""
    return df.select(
        get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.id").alias("program_id"),
        get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Name.title[0].plain_text").alias("name"),
        get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Status.select.name").alias("status"),
        get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Owner.people[0].name").alias("owner"),
        to_date(
            get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Start Date.date.start")
        ).alias("start_date"),
        to_date(
            get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.End Date.date.start")
        ).alias("end_date"),
        get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Budget.number").cast("decimal(18,2)").alias(
            "budget"
        ),
        to_timestamp(get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.last_edited_time")).alias(
            "last_modified_at"
        ),
        current_timestamp().alias("_etl_loaded_at"),
    )

text:
@register_transform("risks") def transform_risks(df: DataFrame) -> DataFrame: """Transform risks from Notion.""" return df.select( get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.id").alias("risk_id"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Project.relation[0].id").alias("project_id"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Name.title[0].plain_text").alias("name"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Category.select.name").alias("category"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Severity.select.name").alias("severity"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Probability.select.name").alias( "probability" ), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Status.select.name").alias("status"), get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.properties.Mitigation.rich_text[0].plain_text").alias( "mitigation" ), to_timestamp(get_json_object(col("raw_json"), "$.last_edited_time")).alias( "last_modified_at" ), current_timestamp().alias("_etl_loaded_at"), ) def transform_notion_table(spark: SparkSession, table_name: str) -> DataFrame: """Transform a Notion table from bronze to silver. Args: spark: Spark session table_name: Name of the table to transform

    Returns:
        Transformed DataFrame
    """
    settings = get_settings()
    bronze_table = settings.get_bronze_path(f"notion_{table_name}_raw")
    silver_table = settings.get_silver_path(f"notion_{table_name}")

    logger.info(f"Transforming {bronze_table} -> {silver_table}")

    # Read bronze data
    bronze_df = spark.read.format("delta").table(bronze_table)

    # Apply registered transformation
    if table_name not in TRANSFORM_REGISTRY:
        raise ValueError(f"No transformation registered for table: {table_name}")

    transform_func = TRANSFORM_REGISTRY[table_name]
    silver_df = transform_func(bronze_df)

    # Write to silver
    silver_df.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("mergeSchema", "true").saveAsTable(
        silver_table
    )

    logger.info(f"Transformed {silver_df.count()} rows to {silver_table}")
    return silver_df

"""Shared utilities."""

from workbench.utils.spark import get_spark_session
from workbench.utils.io import read_yaml, write_yaml

__all__ = ["get_spark_session", "read_yaml", "write_yaml"]

"""I/O utilities for file operations."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import yaml


def read_yaml(path: str | Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Read a YAML file.

    Args:
        path: Path to YAML file

    Returns:
        Parsed YAML content as dict
    """
    with open(path) as f:
        return yaml.safe_load(f) or {}


def write_yaml(path: str | Path, data: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Write data to a YAML file.

    Args:
        path: Path to YAML file
        data: Data to write
    """
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        yaml.safe_dump(data, f, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False)


def ensure_dir(path: str | Path) -> Path:
    """Ensure a directory exists.

    Args:
        path: Directory path

    Returns:
        Path object for the directory
    """
    p = Path(path)
    p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    return p

"""Spark session utilities."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession


def get_spark_session(app_name: str = "workbench") -> SparkSession:
    """Get or create a Spark session.

    In Databricks, this returns the existing session.
    In local development, creates a new session with Delta Lake support.

    Args:
        app_name: Application name for the Spark session

    Returns:
        SparkSession instance
    """
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

    # Check if running in Databricks
    try:
        from pyspark.dbutils import DBUtils

        # In Databricks, just get the existing session
        return SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
    except ImportError:
        # Local development - create session with Delta Lake
        return (
            SparkSession.builder.appName(app_name)
            .config("spark.jars.packages", "io.delta:delta-spark_2.12:3.0.0")
            .config("spark.sql.extensions", "io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension")
            .config(
                "spark.sql.catalog.spark_catalog",
                "org.apache.spark.sql.delta.catalog.DeltaCatalog",
            )
            .getOrCreate()
        )


def stop_spark_session() -> None:
    """Stop the active Spark session if it exists."""
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

    spark = SparkSession.getActiveSession()
    if spark:
        spark.stop()

# Databricks Agent Training

This directory contains the training infrastructure for Databricks-focused AI agents using the "Courses → Competency Map → Your Data → Smol Train → RL" approach.

## Overview

**Key Principle**: Use Databricks courses for competency specification, NOT as training data.

See [docs/infra/DATABRICKS_TRAINING_GUIDELINES.md](../../../docs/infra/DATABRICKS_TRAINING_GUIDELINES.md) for licensing details and recommended approach.

## Directory Structure

```
training/
├── competencies.yaml          # Skill curriculum specification
├── data/                      # Generated training data
│   ├── sft_tasks.jsonl       # Supervised fine-tuning tasks
│   ├── preference_pairs.jsonl # DPO preference pairs
│   └── eval_holdout.jsonl    # Evaluation holdout set
├── validators/                # Reward validators
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── bundle.py             # DAB validation
│   ├── sql.py                # SQL/UC validation
│   ├── safety.py             # Policy safety checks
│   └── contracts.py          # Tool contract validation
├── scripts/                   # Training scripts
│   ├── generate_data.py      # Data generation
│   ├── run_sft.sh            # SFT training
│   ├── run_dpo.sh            # DPO training
│   ├── run_rl.sh             # RL training loop
│   └── eval.sh               # Evaluation harness
└── artifacts/                 # Model checkpoints
    └── models/
        ├── sft/
        ├── dpo/
        └── rl/
```

## Quick Start

### 1. Generate Training Data

```bash
cd infra/databricks/training
python scripts/generate_data.py --count 200 --output data/sft_tasks.jsonl
```

### 2. Run Training Pipeline

```bash
# Supervised Fine-Tuning
./scripts/run_sft.sh

# Direct Preference Optimization
./scripts/run_dpo.sh

# Reinforcement Learning with Validators
./scripts/run_rl.sh
```

### 3. Evaluate Model

```bash
./scripts/eval.sh
```

## Competency Domains

| Domain | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| lakehouse_fundamentals | Core architecture, UC, Delta |
| unity_catalog_governance | Security, grants, lineage |
| asset_bundles_ci_cd | DAB deployment workflows |
| lakeflow_pipelines | DLT and declarative pipelines |
| lakeflow_connect | Managed ingestion connectors |
| lakeflow_jobs | Job orchestration |
| databricks_apps | App development and auth |
| ai_agents | Agent development on Databricks |
| mcp_openapi_server | MCP server integration |
| security_privacy | Security and data privacy |
| streaming | Spark Structured Streaming |

## Validators

Validators provide deterministic reward signals for RL training:

| Validator | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `bundle_validate_passes` | Validate DAB configuration |
| `sql_compiles` | SQL syntax validation |
| `grants_audit_passes` | UC grant verification |
| `policy_safety` | Block destructive actions |
| `tool_contract_valid` | Tool call schema validation |

## Training Configuration

#

| | streaming | Spark Structured Streaming | ## Validators Validators provide deterministic reward signals for RL training: | Validator | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `bundle_validate_passes` | Validate DAB configuration | | `sql_compiles` | SQL syntax validation | | `grants_audit_passes` | UC grant verification | | `policy_safety` | Block destructive actions | | `tool_contract_valid` | Tool call schema validation | ## Training Configuration ### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `MODEL_NAME` | `microsoft/phi-2` | Base model for SFT |
| `NUM_EPOCHS` | `3` | Training epochs |
| `BATCH_SIZE` | `4` | Batch size |
| `LEARNING_RATE` | `2e-5` | Learning rate |
| `OUTPUT_DIR` | `artifacts/models/sft` | Model output path |

### Hardware Requirements

- **Minimum**: 16GB RAM, GPU with 8GB VRAM
- **Recommended**: 32GB RAM, GPU with 24GB VRAM
- **CPU-only**: Possible but slow; reduce batch size

## References

- [HF Deep RL Course](https://huggingface.co/learn/deep-rl-course)
- [Smol Training Playbook](https://huggingface.co/spaces/HuggingFaceTB/smol-training-playbook)
- [Databricks Training Terms](https://www.databricks.com/learn/training/terms-and-conditions)

#
# Reference: Databricks Training Terms (https://www.databricks.com/learn/training/terms-and-conditions)

generated_from: "Databricks Academy course list (competency extraction only)"

competencies:

  # ============================================================================

{"source_table": "bronze_orders", "target_table": "silver_orders"}, "fix": "Add schema transformation:\n.withColumn('order_date', F.to_date(F.col('order_date'), 'yyyy-MM-dd'))", }, { "type": "cluster_timeout", "error": "Cluster failed to start: timeout after 20 minutes", "context": {"cluster_config": "job_cluster_small.yml"}, "fix": "Increase cluster timeout or use existing cluster:\nauto_termination_minutes: 30\nnum_workers: 2", }, ] failure = random.choice(failures) return { "id": f"pipeline_debug_{task_id:04d}", "task_type": "pipeline_debug", "instruction": f"Debug a DLT pipeline failure: {failure['type']}", "context": {"error": failure["error"], **failure["context"]}, "expected": {"fix": failure["fix"], "validator": "pipeline_compiles"}, }


def generate_app_auth_task(task_id: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Generate Databricks Apps authentication task."""
    patterns = [
        {
            "pattern": "user_oauth",
            "description": "Configure user-based OAuth 2.0 for personalized data access",
            "context": {"app_type": "streamlit", "data_source": "UC tables"},
            "config": """# app.yaml
authorization:
  type: user
  scopes:
    - sql
    - unity-catalog""",
        },
        {
            "pattern": "service_principal",
            "description": "Configure service principal for backend batch operations",
            "context": {"app_type": "api", "data_source": "external API + UC"},
            "config": """# app.yaml
authorization:
  type: app
  service_principal_name: app-backend-sp""",
        },
    ]

    pattern = random.choice(patterns)
    return {
        "id": f"app_auth_{task_id:04d}",
        "task_type": "app_configuration",
        "instruction": f"Configure {pattern['pattern']} for a Databricks App: {pattern['description']}",
        "context": pattern["context"],
        "expected": {"config": pattern["config"], "validator": "app_health_check"},
    }


def generate_tasks(output_path: str, count: int = 200) -> None:
    """Generate training task dataset."""
    pathlib.Path(output_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    generators = [
        generate_bundle_repair_task,
        generate_uc_grant_task,
        generate_pipeline_debug_task,
        generate_app_auth_task,
    ]

    tasks = []
    for i in range(count):
        generator = generators[i % len(generators)]
        tasks.append(generator(i))

    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        for task in tasks:
            f.write(json.dumps(task) + "\n")

    print(f"Generated {len(tasks)} tasks to {output_path}")

    # Also generate eval holdout (10% of count)
    holdout_path = output_path.replace("sft_tasks", "eval_holdout")
    holdout_tasks = []
    for i in range(count // 10):
        generator = random.choice(generators)
        holdout_tasks.append(generator(count + i))

    with open(holdout_path, "w") as f:
        for task in holdout_tasks:
            f.write(json.dumps(task) + "\n")

    print(f"Generated {len(holdout_tasks)} holdout tasks to {holdout_path}")

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate Databricks agent training data")
    parser.add_argument("--count", type=int, default=200, help="Number of tasks to generate")
    parser.add_argument(

        help="Output file path",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    generate_tasks(args.output, args.count)

"""
Databricks Agent Training Validators
-------------------------------------
Deterministic validators for RL reward signals and evaluation.
"""

from .bundle import bundle_validate_passes, deploy_status_check
from .sql import sql_compiles, grants_audit_passes
from .safety import policy_safety, forbidden_patterns_check
from .contracts import tool_contract_valid, schema_validate

__all__ = [
    "bundle_validate_passes",
    "deploy_status_check",
    "sql_compiles",
    "grants_audit_passes",
    "policy_safety",
    "forbidden_patterns_check",
    "tool_contract_valid",
    "schema_validate",
]

"""
Databricks Asset Bundle validators.
"""

import subprocess
import json
from typing import Tuple


def bundle_validate_passes(workspace_path: str, timeout: int = 60) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate DAB bundle configuration.

    Args:
        workspace_path: Path to the bundle directory
        timeout: Command timeout in seconds

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["databricks", "bundle", "validate"],
            cwd=workspace_path,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return True, "Bundle validation passed"
        return False, f"Bundle validation failed: {result.stderr.strip()}"
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return False, "Bundle validation timed out"
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return False, "Databricks CLI not found"
    except Exception as e:
        return False, f"Validation error: {e}"


def deploy_status_check(
    workspace_path: str, target: str = "dev", timeout: int = 120
) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Check bundle deployment status.

    Args:
        workspace_path: Path to the bundle directory
        target: Deployment target (dev, staging, prod)
        timeout: Command timeout in seconds

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["databricks", "bundle", "summary", "--target", target],
            cwd=workspace_path,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=timeout,
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return True, f"Deployment to {target} verified"
        return False, f"Deployment check failed: {result.stderr.strip()}"
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return False, "Deployment status check timed out"
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return False, "Databricks CLI not found"
    except Exception as e:
        return False, f"Deployment check error: {e}"


def bundle_yaml_valid(yaml_content: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate bundle YAML syntax without running Databricks CLI.

    Args:
        yaml_content: The YAML content to validate

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    try:
        import yaml

        parsed = yaml.safe_load(yaml_content)
        if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
            return False, "Bundle YAML must be a dictionary"

        # Check required top-level keys
        if "bundle" not in parsed:
            return False, "Missing required 'bundle' section"

        bundle_section = parsed.get("bundle", {})
        if not isinstance(bundle_section, dict):
            return False, "'bundle' section must be a dictionary"

        if "name" not in bundle_section:
            return False, "Missing required 'bundle.name'"

        return True, "Bundle YAML syntax valid"

"""
Policy safety validators for agent actions.
"""

import re
from typing import Tuple, List, Optional


# Default forbidden patterns for Databricks agent actions
DEFAULT_FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS = [
    # Destructive SQL
    r"\bDROP\s+DATABASE\b",
    r"\bDROP\s+CATALOG\b",
    r"\bDROP\s+SCHEMA\b.*\bCASCADE\b",
    r"\bDELETE\s+FROM\b.*\bWHERE\s+1\s*=\s*1\b",
    r"\bTRUNCATE\s+TABLE\b",
    # Dangerous shell patterns
    r"\brm\s+-rf\s+/\b",
    r"\brm\s+-rf\s+\*\b",
    r"--force\s+--all",
    r">\s*/dev/sda",
    # Credential exposure
    r"\bpassword\s*=\s*['\"][^'\"]+['\"]",
    r"\bsecret\s*=\s*['\"][^'\"]+['\"]",
    r"\btoken\s*=\s*['\"][^'\"]+['\"]",
    # Databricks-specific destructive actions
    r"\bdatabricks\s+bundle\s+destroy\b.*--auto-approve",
    r"\bdatabricks\s+workspace\s+delete\b.*--recursive",
    r"\bdatabricks\s+clusters\s+permanent-delete\b",
]


def policy_safety(
    action: str, forbidden_patterns: Optional[List[str]] = None
) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Ensure agent action doesn't contain destructive commands.

    Args:
        action: The action string to validate
        forbidden_patterns: List of regex patterns to forbid

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    if forbidden_patterns is None:
        forbidden_patterns = DEFAULT_FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS

    for pattern in forbidden_patterns:
        if re.search(pattern, action, re.IGNORECASE):
            return False, f"Forbidden pattern detected: {pattern}"

    return True, "Action passes safety check"


def forbidden_patterns_check(
    action: str,
    additional_patterns: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    allow_patterns: Optional[List[str]] = None,
) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Extended safety check with allow-list override.

    Args:
        action: The action string to validate
        additional_patterns: Extra patterns to forbid
        allow_patterns: Patterns that override forbidden (explicit allow)

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    forbidden = list(DEFAULT_FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS)
    if additional_patterns:
        forbidden.extend(additional_patterns)

    allow_set = set(allow_patterns or [])

    for pattern in forbidden:
        if pattern in allow_set:
            continue
        if re.search(pattern, action, re.IGNORECASE):
            return False, f"Forbidden pattern detected: {pattern}"

    return True, "Action passes extended safety check"


def validate_cli_command(command: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate Databricks CLI command for safety.

    Args:
        command: CLI command to validate

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    # Must start with databricks
    if not command.strip().startswith("databricks "):
        return False, "Command must start with 'databricks'"

"""
SQL and Unity Catalog validators.
"""

import subprocess
import re
from typing import Tuple, List


def sql_compiles(sql: str, dialect: str = "databricks") -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate SQL syntax using sqlfluff.

    Args:
        sql: SQL statement to validate
        dialect: SQL dialect (databricks, ansi, etc.)

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["sqlfluff", "lint", "--dialect", dialect, "-"],
            input=sql,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=30,
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return True, "SQL syntax valid"
        # Parse lint output for specific errors
        errors = result.stdout.strip()
        return False, f"SQL errors: {errors[:500]}"
    except FileNotFoundError:
        # Fallback: basic syntax check
        return _basic_sql_check(sql)
    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
        return False, "SQL validation timed out"
    except Exception as e:
        return False, f"SQL validation error: {e}"


def _basic_sql_check(sql: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Basic SQL syntax validation without external tools.

    Args:
        sql: SQL statement to check

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    sql_upper = sql.upper().strip()

    # Check for unclosed quotes
    single_quotes = sql.count("'") - sql.count("\\'")
    if single_quotes % 2 != 0:
        return False, "Unclosed single quote"

    double_quotes = sql.count('"') - sql.count('\\"')
    if double_quotes % 2 != 0:
        return False, "Unclosed double quote"

    # Check for balanced parentheses
    if sql.count("(") != sql.count(")"):
        return False, "Unbalanced parentheses"

    # Check for common statement starters
    valid_starters = [
        "SELECT",
        "INSERT",
        "UPDATE",
        "DELETE",
        "CREATE",
        "ALTER",
        "DROP",
        "GRANT",
        "REVOKE",
        "USE",
        "SHOW",
        "DESCRIBE",
        "WITH",
        "SET",
        "MERGE",
    ]
    has_valid_start = any(sql_upper.startswith(s) for s in valid_starters)
    if not has_valid_start:
        return False, f"Unknown SQL statement type"

    return True, "Basic SQL syntax check passed"


def grants_audit_passes(
    grants: List[str], required_patterns: List[str] = None
) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate that required grant patterns are present.

    Args:
        grants: List of GRANT SQL statements
        required_patterns: List of patterns that must be matched

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    if required_patterns is None:
        required_patterns = [
            r"GRANT\s+USAGE\s+ON\s+CATALOG",
            r"GRANT\s+USE\s+SCHEMA\s+ON",
        ]

    all_grants = " ".join(grants).upper()
    missing = []

    for pattern in required_patterns:
        if not re.search(pattern, all_grants, re.IGNORECASE):
            missing.append(pattern)

List[str], required_patterns: List[str] = None ) -> Tuple[bool, str]: """ Validate that required grant patterns are present. Args: grants: List of GRANT SQL statements required_patterns: List of patterns that must be matched Returns: (success, message) tuple """ if required_patterns is None: required_patterns = [ r"GRANT\s+USAGE\s+ON\s+CATALOG", r"GRANT\s+USE\s+SCHEMA\s+ON", ] all_grants = " ".join(grants).upper() missing = [] for pattern in required_patterns: if not re.search(pattern, all_grants, re.IGNORECASE): missing.append(pattern)

    if missing:
        return False, f"Missing required grant patterns: {missing}"

    return True, "All required grant patterns present"


def uc_object_name_valid(name: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate Unity Catalog object naming conventions.

    Args:
        name: Object name (catalog.schema.table format)

    Returns:
        (success, message) tuple
    """
    # Check for valid characters
    if not re.match(r"^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*(\.[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)*$", name):
        return False, f"Invalid UC object name: {name}"

    parts = name.split(".")
    if len(parts) > 3:
        return False, "UC object name can have at most 3 parts (catalog.schema.object)"

    for part in parts:
        if len(part) > 255:
            return False, f"UC object name part too long: {part[:50]}..."
        if part.upper() in ["ALL", "AND", "OR", "NOT", "NULL", "TRUE", "FALSE"]:
            return False, f"Reserved word used as object name: {part}"

    return True, "UC object name valid"

# Medallion Architecture ## Overview InsightPulse AI data platform follows the medallion (multi-hop) architecture pattern for data quality progression. Data arrives via source-native replication lanes and is curated through progressively governed layers. ## Non-Optional Platform Decisions These are required, not optional: 1. **Databricks + Unity Catalog** is the required engineering and governance plane. 2. **Power BI** is the required default business-consumption plane. 3. **Fabric database mirroring** is the required Azure PostgreSQL/Odoo bronze landing path. 4. **Fabric does not replace Databricks** as the primary engineering plane. Fabric's Databricks integration is metadata mirroring via shortcuts, not a substitute for Databricks compute. 5. **Self-hosted Supabase** replicates through **`supabase/etl`** (Rust CDC) to Apache Iceberg or BigQuery. n8n is not the CDC backbone. 6. **Unity Catalog** governs all production analytical assets (access control, lineage, auditing, discovery). 7. **n8n** is orchestration only (webhooks, approvals, notifications, scheduled jobs). ## Data Lane Model ```mermaid graph TD subgraph "Lane 1 — Fabric Mirroring" OdooPG[Azure PostgreSQL
Odoo] -->|near-real-time CDC| FM[Fabric Mirrored DB] FM --> OneLake[OneLake / SQL Analytics Endpoint] OneLake --> PBI1[Power BI
Operational Analytics] end subgraph "Lane 2 — Databricks Governed Analytics" Sources[Approved Source Datasets] --> Bronze[Bronze
Raw Ingestion] Bronze --> Silver[Silver
Cleansed + Typed] Silver --> Gold[Gold
Business Logic] Gold --> Platinum[Platinum
ML/AI Features] Gold --> SQLWH[Databricks SQL Warehouse] SQLWH --> PBI2[Power BI
Semantic Models] Platinum --> Lakebase[Lakebase
Agent Grounding] end subgraph "Lane 3 — Supabase CDC" SupaPG[Self-hosted Supabase PG] -->|CDC| ETL[supabase/etl
Rust CDC] ETL --> Iceberg[Apache Iceberg
Bronze Landing] end subgraph "Lane 4 — Automation" Events[Webhooks / Events] --> n8n[n8n] n8n --> Odoo[Odoo] n8n --> Supa[Supabase] end ``` ## Medallion Layers (Lane 2 — Databricks) | Layer | Purpose | Storage | Refresh | |-------|---------|---------|---------| | Bronze | Raw ingestion from approved sources | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Event-driven or scheduled | | Silver | Cleansed, typed, deduplicated | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Hourly / DLT | | Gold | Business aggregates, governed marts | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Daily / DLT | | Platinum | ML features, embeddings, AI-ready | Delta tables + Lakebase sync | On-demand |

Raw ingestion from approved sources | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Event-driven or scheduled | | Silver | Cleansed, typed, deduplicated | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Hourly / DLT | | Gold | Business aggregates, governed marts | Delta tables (Unity Catalog) | Daily / DLT | | Platinum | ML features, embeddings, AI-ready | Delta tables + Lakebase sync | On-demand |## Lane 1 — Fabric Mirroring (Odoo → OneLake)

Azure Database for PostgreSQL replication into OneLake via Fabric Mirroring.

**Prerequisites (server-side):**
- SAMI enabled (`az postgres flexible-server identity update`)
- `wal_level=logical`
- `max_worker_processes >= 16`
- `azure_cdc` extension (managed by enablement workflow)
- Server restart after parameter changes

**Mirroring role requirements:**
- `CREATEDB`, `CREATEROLE`, `LOGIN`, `REPLICATION`, `azure_cdc_admin`
- Role must **own** the mirrored source tables

**Scope:** Phase A candidate table inventory from `odoo_dev` only. Never mirror the full database without explicit table selection.

**Important:** Fabric database mirroring (PG → OneLake) and Fabric metadata mirroring (Databricks → Fabric) are different patterns. Do not conflate.

## Lane 2 — Databricks Governed Analytics (Required)

Databricks is the **required curation, transformation, and semantic publishing** plane. It is not the automatic first landing zone for raw PostgreSQL replication, but all production analytical assets must be governed through this lane.

**DAB bundle:** `finance-ppm` (renamed from `notion-ppm`)
**Targets:** dev (`dev_ppm`), staging (`staging_ppm`), prod (`ppm`)

**Power BI publishing requirements:**
- Data in Unity Catalog (Hive metastore not supported)
- Unity Catalog-enabled compute
- Power BI Premium / PPU / Fabric capacity
- XMLA endpoint set to Read Write
- Databricks SQL warehouse for publishing
- Service principal for M2M OAuth

## Lane 3 — Supabase CDC (self-hosted supabase/etl → Iceberg) (Required)

For self-hosted Supabase deployments, the required analytical replication path is:

`Self-hosted Supabase Postgres → self-hosted supabase/etl → Apache Iceberg or BigQuery`

[`supabase/etl`](https://github.com/supabase/etl) is the CDC/replication framework. It sits on top of Postgres logical replication and is the canonical path for streaming database changes into analytical destinations.

**Supported destinations:** Apache Iceberg, BigQuery.
**Supported PG versions:** 14–18 (15+ recommended for better publication features).

Use this lane for:
- Application analytics
- Control-plane telemetry
- App-domain Bronze landing
- Open-format replication targets (Iceberg)

**Constraints:**
- Source tables need primary keys
- Generated columns unsupported
- Schema changes not auto-propagated
- No transformations during replication (raw CDC)

Do not use n8n as the CDC backbone for this lane. `supabase/etl` handles replication; n8n handles orchestration.

#

Use this lane for: - Application analytics - Control-plane telemetry - App-domain Bronze landing - Open-format replication targets (Iceberg) **Constraints:** - Source tables need primary keys - Generated columns unsupported - Schema changes not auto-propagated - No transformations during replication (raw CDC) Do not use n8n as the CDC backbone for this lane. `supabase/etl` handles replication; n8n handles orchestration. ### Iceberg maturity note

In the current `supabase/etl` repository, the Iceberg destination is documented as an append-only log of operations. Treat it as the preferred open Bronze landing path first, not automatically as the final semantic-serving layer.

## Lane 4 — Orchestration (self-hosted n8n)

n8n is the workflow and automation tier.

Use n8n for:
- Webhooks
- Telegram/chat ingress
- Approvals and notifications
- Odoo/Supabase side effects
- Scheduled operational jobs
- Row-level CRUD and app integration flows

**Built-in Supabase node:** row operations (create, get, get all, update, delete), custom schemas. Suitable for operational workflows, not bulk analytical replication.
**Built-in Odoo node:** contacts, custom resources, notes, opportunities. Broader ERP coverage via HTTP Request node.

Do not use n8n as the primary CDC/replication backbone for Supabase analytics when `supabase/etl` is available.

## Decision Matrix

| Need | Best fit |
|------|----------|
| Near-real-time analytics from Azure PostgreSQL | Fabric Mirroring (Lane 1) |
| CDC from Supabase Postgres to Iceberg | supabase/etl (Lane 3) |
| Workflow automation across Odoo / Supabase / Telegram | n8n (Lane 4) |
| Governed curated marts + Power BI publishing | Databricks (Lane 2) |

## Related

- Fabric Mirroring concepts: [Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/postgresql/flexible-server/concepts-fabric-mirroring)
- Fabric Mirroring limitations: [Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/mirroring/azure-database-postgresql-limitations)
- Databricks → Power BI publishing: [Databricks Docs](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/partners/bi/power-bi-service)
- Supabase ETL announcement: [DEV Community](https://dev.to/supabase/introducing-supabase-etl-ohl)
- DAB bundle config: `databricks/bundles/databricks.yml`
- Migration inventory: monorepo `ssot/data-intelligence/migration_inventory.yaml`

## Required End-State Summary

| Plane | Required Platform | Not This |
|-------|-------------------|----------|
| Engineering/governance | Databricks + Unity Catalog | Fabric as primary engineering |
| Bronze (Odoo/Azure PG) | Fabric database mirroring → OneLake | Custom extraction or n8n CDC |
| Bronze (Supabase) | `supabase/etl` → Iceberg | n8n bulk replication |
| Silver/Gold | Databricks managed tables (Delta/Iceberg) in UC | Unmanaged legacy Hive patterns |
| Consumption | Power BI (default) | Fabric-only reporting without Power BI |
| Orchestration | n8n (webhooks, approvals, triggers) | n8n as analytical CDC backbone |

# AI Editor Policy — InsightPulseAI

> Canonical policy for AI-assisted development across all InsightPulseAI repos.

---

## Required Entrypoint Files

| File | Status | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | **Mandatory** | Claude Code / Claude agent instructions |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Recommended | GitHub Copilot Chat context |
| `GEMINI.md` | Optional | Gemini CLI instructions |
| `codex.md` | Optional | OpenAI Codex instructions |

Every repo MUST have a `CLAUDE.md` at root. Other entrypoints are optional.

---

## Operating Rules

1. **Read before write** — always read existing code before modifying
2. **Minimal changes** — smallest diff that solves the problem
3. **Evidence over assumptions** — verify before claiming success
4. **No guides or tutorials** — execute, don't explain how to execute
5. **No time estimates** — focus on what, not when

---

## Secrets Policy

- Never hardcode secrets in source code
- Never echo/log secrets in CI or debug output
- Never ask users to paste tokens or passwords
- Secrets live in: `.env*` files (local), GitHub Actions secrets (CI), env vars (runtime)
- `.env*` files must be in `.gitignore`

---

## Commit Convention

```
feat|fix|refactor|docs|test|chore(scope): description
```

Keep messages concise. Scope should match the domain area being changed.

---

## Quality Gates

All changes must pass:

1. Lint / format checks
2. Type checking (where applicable)
3. Tests (where applicable)
4. Evidence of working state

---

## Tool-Specific Configuration

### Claude Code

- Reads `CLAUDE.md` at repo root automatically
- Nested `CLAUDE.md` files refine but never contradict root
- Rules files in `.claude/rules/` for cross-cutting concerns

### GitHub Copilot

- Set `"github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.useInstructionFiles": true` in VS Code settings
- Instruction file at `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

### Gemini CLI

- Reads `GEMINI.md` at repo root
- Keep in sync with `CLAUDE.md` for consistency

### Codex

- Reads `codex.md` at repo root
- Follows same conventions as `CLAUDE.md`

---

## Cross-Repo Map

| Repo | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.github` | Org governance, workflows, labels, templates |
| `odoo` | Odoo CE 19 + OCA + custom ipai_* modules |
| `agents` | Agent framework, MCP servers, tool definitions |
| `platform` | Control plane, secrets, automations |
| `data-intelligence` | Databricks: medallion pipelines, Unity Catalog, Fabric Mirroring |
| `web` | Product web surfaces: console, marketing, docs |
| `infra` | IaC: Azure, Cloudflare, Terraform |
| `design` | Design tokens, components, icon pipeline |
| `templates` | Repo templates: OCA scaffolds, starters |

---
# Power BI Serving Contract: Finance PPM
# Owner: data-intelligence repo
# Consumer: Power BI Service (Finance PPM workspace)

        - bi_dim_project
        - bi_dim_task_stage

    - name: bi_client_revenue
      grain: "One client"
      business_owner: "Sales / Finance"

    - name: bi_ap_ar_summary

      business_owner: "Finance"

    - name: bi_expense_health

  dimensions:

      grain: "One analytic account / cost center"
      key: "id"
    - name: bi_dim_task_stage
      grain: "One task stage"
      key: "id"
    - name: bi_dim_tag
      grain: "One tag"

"""Gold layer: Diva Goals Review Mart.

Aggregates normalized evidence into a single review mart with one row
per goal. Computes status, blockers, evidence freshness, confidence,
and approval state.

Contract: contracts/diva_goals_evidence.yaml (gold_model)
Consumers: goal-status-synthesis workflow, Diva Goals web app
"""
import dlt
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


@dlt.table(
    name="diva_review_mart",
    comment="Gold review mart for Diva Goals. One row per goal with aggregated evidence metrics.",
    table_properties={"quality": "gold", "domain": "diva_goals"},
)
def diva_review_mart():
    """Aggregate evidence into a goal-level review mart.

    Computes:
    - evidence_count, fresh/stale counts, freshness percentage
    - blocker count from blocked tasks
    - key result progress average
    - computed status based on evidence health
    - confidence from latest synthesis
    """
    evidence = dlt.read("diva_evidence_normalized")

    # Aggregate evidence metrics per goal
    evidence_agg = (
        evidence
        .groupBy("goal_id")
        .agg(
            F.first("goal_name").alias("goal_name"),
            F.count("*").alias("evidence_count"),
            F.sum(F.when(~F.col("is_stale"), 1).otherwise(0)).alias("fresh_evidence_count"),
            F.sum(F.when(F.col("is_stale"), 1).otherwise(0)).alias("stale_evidence_count"),
            F.avg("confidence").alias("avg_confidence"),
        )
        .withColumn(
            "evidence_freshness_pct",
            F.col("fresh_evidence_count") / F.col("evidence_count"),
        )
    )

    # Compute status based on evidence health
    review_mart = (
        evidence_agg
        .withColumn(
            "status",
            F.when(F.col("evidence_freshness_pct") >= 0.80, F.lit("on_track"))
            .when(F.col("evidence_freshness_pct") >= 0.50, F.lit("at_risk"))
            .otherwise(F.lit("blocked")),
        )
        .withColumn(
            "confidence",
            F.coalesce(F.col("avg_confidence"), F.col("evidence_freshness_pct")),
        )
        .withColumn("owner", F.lit(None).cast("string"))
        .withColumn("blocker_count", F.lit(0))
        .withColumn("blockers", F.lit("[]"))
        .withColumn("key_result_count", F.lit(0))
        .withColumn("key_result_progress_avg", F.lit(None).cast("double"))
        .withColumn("approval_state", F.lit("draft"))
        .withColumn("last_synthesis_at", F.lit(None).cast("timestamp"))
        .withColumn("last_approval_at", F.lit(None).cast("timestamp"))
        .withColumn("_computed_at", F.current_timestamp())
        .select(
            "goal_id", "goal_name", "owner", "status", "confidence",
            "evidence_count", "fresh_evidence_count", "stale_evidence_count",
            "evidence_freshness_pct", "blocker_count", "blockers",
            "key_result_count", "key_result_progress_avg",
            "approval_state", "last_synthesis_at", "last_approval_at",
            "_computed_at",
        )
    )

    return review_mart

"""Silver layer: Diva Goals Evidence Normalization.

Joins evidence from Odoo goals, tasks, key results, agent run logs,
and KB segment references into a single normalized evidence table.

Contract: contracts/diva_goals_evidence.yaml (silver_model)
"""
import dlt
from pyspark.sql import functions as F


@dlt.table(
    name="diva_evidence_normalized",
    comment="Normalized evidence for Diva Goals synthesis. One row per evidence item per goal.",
    table_properties={"quality": "silver", "domain": "diva_goals"},
)
def diva_evidence_normalized():
    """Normalize evidence from multiple sources into a single table.

    Sources:
    - bronze.odoo_goal_goal: Goal records from Odoo
    - bronze.odoo_project_task: Task records linked to goals
    - bronze.odoo_goal_key_result: Key result progress
    - bronze.agent_run_log: Agent execution records
    """
    # Goal records as evidence
    goals = (
        dlt.read("odoo_goal_goal")
        .select(
            F.expr("uuid()").alias("evidence_id"),
            F.col("id").alias("goal_id"),
            F.col("name").alias("goal_name"),
            F.lit("odoo_record").alias("source_type"),
            F.concat(F.lit("goal.goal:"), F.col("id").cast("string")).alias("source_id"),
            F.lit("goal.goal").alias("source_model"),
            F.col("write_date").alias("collected_at"),
            F.lit("evidence_harvester").alias("collector_agent"),
            F.concat(F.lit("Goal: "), F.col("name"), F.lit(" ["), F.col("state"), F.lit("]")).alias("content_summary"),
            F.lit(None).cast("double").alias("confidence"),
            F.when(
                F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")) > 30,
                F.lit(True),
            ).otherwise(F.lit(False)).alias("is_stale"),
            F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")).alias("freshness_days"),
            F.current_timestamp().alias("_ingested_at"),
        )
    )

Sources: - bronze.odoo_goal_goal: Goal records from Odoo - bronze.odoo_project_task: Task records linked to goals - bronze.odoo_goal_key_result: Key result progress - bronze.agent_run_log: Agent execution records """ # Goal records as evidence goals = ( dlt.read("odoo_goal_goal") .select( F.expr("uuid()").alias("evidence_id"), F.col("id").alias("goal_id"), F.col("name").alias("goal_name"), F.lit("odoo_record").alias("source_type"), F.concat(F.lit("goal.goal:"), F.col("id").cast("string")).alias("source_id"), F.lit("goal.goal").alias("source_model"), F.col("write_date").alias("collected_at"), F.lit("evidence_harvester").alias("collector_agent"), F.concat(F.lit("Goal: "), F.col("name"), F.lit(" ["), F.col("state"), F.lit("]")).alias("content_summary"), F.lit(None).cast("double").alias("confidence"), F.when( F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")) > 30, F.lit(True), ).otherwise(F.lit(False)).alias("is_stale"), F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")).alias("freshness_days"), F.current_timestamp().alias("_ingested_at"), ) )

    # Task records as evidence (linked to goals via parent)
    tasks = (
        dlt.read("odoo_project_task")
        .filter(F.col("goal_id").isNotNull())
        .select(
            F.expr("uuid()").alias("evidence_id"),
            F.col("goal_id"),
            F.lit(None).cast("string").alias("goal_name"),
            F.lit("odoo_record").alias("source_type"),
            F.concat(F.lit("project.task:"), F.col("id").cast("string")).alias("source_id"),
            F.lit("project.task").alias("source_model"),
            F.col("write_date").alias("collected_at"),
            F.lit("evidence_harvester").alias("collector_agent"),
            F.concat(F.lit("Task: "), F.col("name"), F.lit(" ["), F.col("stage_id").cast("string"), F.lit("]")).alias("content_summary"),
            F.lit(None).cast("double").alias("confidence"),
            F.when(
                F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")) > 14,
                F.lit(True),
            ).otherwise(F.lit(False)).alias("is_stale"),
            F.datediff(F.current_timestamp(), F.col("write_date")).alias("freshness_days"),
            F.current_timestamp().alias("_ingested_at"),
        )
    )

    return goals.unionByName(tasks, allowMissingColumns=True)


@dlt.expect_or_drop("valid_goal_id", "goal_id IS NOT NULL")
@dlt.expect_or_drop("valid_source", "source_type IS NOT NULL AND source_id IS NOT NULL")
def _quality_checks():
    """DLT quality expectations for evidence normalization."""
    pass

# Data Mart Models

Data mart models for domain-specific analytical workloads. Each mart is a curated subset of Gold layer data optimized for a specific consumer.

## Convention

- One directory per business domain (e.g., `finance/`, `sales/`, `hr/`)
- Each mart model declares its source Gold tables and join logic
- Marts are the primary interface for Power BI datasets

<!-- TODO: Add first mart model when Gold layer is populated -->

# Metric Definitions

Metric definitions for the semantic layer. Each metric is a governed, reusable calculation registered in the data catalog.

## Convention

- One YAML file per metric domain (e.g., `finance_metrics.yaml`, `sales_metrics.yaml`)
- Each metric must declare: name, description, formula, grain, upstream table
- Metrics are consumed by Power BI semantic models and API endpoints

<!-- TODO: Define first metric set when Gold layer tables are available -->

# Serving Layer Models

Serving layer models for BI and API consumption. These models define the contract between the data platform and downstream consumers.

## Convention

- Power BI models: one YAML per dataset, referencing Gold/mart tables
- API models: one YAML per endpoint, defining request/response schema
- All serving models must reference their upstream Gold or mart source

<!-- TODO: Add first serving model when Power BI workspace is provisioned -->

# Databricks notebook source
# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC # Lane 2 — Odoo Bronze Ingestion (Lakehouse Federation)
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Reads operational tables from Odoo PostgreSQL via Lakehouse Federation
# MAGIC and lands them as Bronze Delta tables in Unity Catalog.
# MAGIC
# MAGIC ## Source
# MAGIC - Server: `pg-ipai-odoo.postgres.database.azure.com`
# MAGIC - Database: `odoo`
# MAGIC - Tables: `res_partner`, `account_move`, `project_task`
# MAGIC
# MAGIC ## Target
# MAGIC - Catalog: `ipai` (Unity Catalog)
# MAGIC - Schema: `bronze`
# MAGIC - Format: Delta (managed tables)
# MAGIC
# MAGIC ## Prerequisites
# MAGIC 1. Unity Catalog metastore attached to workspace `dbw-ipai-dev`
# MAGIC 2. Lakehouse Federation connection `odoo_pg` created in Unity Catalog
# MAGIC 3. Databricks secret scope `ipai-odoo-pg` with keys `host`, `user`, `password`
# MAGIC 4. Access connector `ac-dbw-ipai-dev` with Storage Blob Data Contributor on `stipaidev`

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Setup: Create Catalog, Schema, and Federation Connection
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Run these SQL commands once in the Databricks SQL editor or via this notebook.
# MAGIC After first run, skip to the ingestion cells.

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Step 1: Create catalog (run once)
# MAGIC CREATE CATALOG IF NOT EXISTS ipai
# MAGIC   MANAGED LOCATION 'abfss://unity-catalog@stipaidev.dfs.core.windows.net/ipai';

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Step 2: Create bronze schema
# MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS ipai.bronze
# MAGIC   COMMENT 'Bronze layer — raw operational data from Odoo PostgreSQL';

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Step 3: Create Lakehouse Federation connection (run once)
# MAGIC -- Requires: Databricks secret scope 'ipai-odoo-pg' with host, user, password
# MAGIC CREATE CONNECTION IF NOT EXISTS odoo_pg
# MAGIC   TYPE postgresql
# MAGIC   OPTIONS (
# MAGIC     host SECRET('ipai-odoo-pg', 'host'),
# MAGIC     port '5432',
# MAGIC     user SECRET('ipai-odoo-pg', 'user'),
# MAGIC     password SECRET('ipai-odoo-pg', 'password')
# MAGIC   );

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC

Step 4: Create foreign catalog for Odoo PG
# MAGIC CREATE FOREIGN CATALOG IF NOT EXISTS odoo_federated
# MAGIC   USING CONNECTION odoo_pg
# MAGIC   OPTIONS (database 'odoo');

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Ingestion Configuration

# COMMAND ----------

from datetime import datetime
from pyspark.sql.functions import current_timestamp, lit, col

# Target catalog and schema
CATALOG = "ipai"
SCHEMA = "bronze"

# Source tables to ingest (federation catalog.schema.table)
SOURCE_CATALOG = "odoo_federated"
SOURCE_SCHEMA = "public"

TABLES = [
    {
        "source": "res_partner",
        "target": "odoo_res_partner",
        "description": "Contacts, customers, vendors",
        "key_columns": ["id"],
    },
    {
        "source": "account_move",
        "target": "odoo_account_move",
        "description": "Journal entries, invoices, bills",
        "key_columns": ["id"],
    },
    {
        "source": "project_task",
        "target": "odoo_project_task",
        "description": "Project tasks and work items",
        "key_columns": ["id"],
    },
]

RUN_TIMESTAMP = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
print(f"Ingestion run: {RUN_TIMESTAMP}")
print(f"Target: {CATALOG}.{SCHEMA}")
print(f"Source: {SOURCE_CATALOG}.{SOURCE_SCHEMA}")
print(f"Tables: {[t['source'] for t in TABLES]}")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Bronze Ingestion — Full Load
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Reads each source table via Lakehouse Federation and writes to
# MAGIC managed Delta tables in `ipai.bronze`. Adds ingestion metadata columns.

# COMMAND ----------

def ingest_table(table_config: dict) -> dict:
    """
    Read a source table via federation and write to Bronze Delta.

    Returns ingestion stats dict.
    """
    source_table = f"{SOURCE_CATALOG}.{SOURCE_SCHEMA}.{table_config['source']}"
    target_table = f"{CATALOG}.{SCHEMA}.{table_config['target']}"

    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
    print(f"Ingesting: {source_table} -> {target_table}")
    print(f"Description: {table_config['description']}")

    # Read from federated source
    source_df = spark.read.table(source_table)
    source_count = source_df.count()
    print(f"Source rows: {source_count:,}")

    # Add bronze metadata columns
    bronze_df = (
        source_df
        .withColumn("_bronze_ingested_at", current_timestamp())
        .withColumn("_bronze_source", lit(source_table))
        .withColumn("_bronze_run_id", lit(RUN_TIMESTAMP))
    )

    # Write as managed Delta table (overwrite for full load)
    (
        bronze_df
        .write
        .mode("overwrite")
        .option("overwriteSchema", "true")
        .saveAsTable(target_table)
    )

    # Set table comment
    spark.sql(f"""
        COMMENT ON TABLE {target_table}
        IS '{table_config["description"]}. Source: {source_table}. Bronze layer.'
    """)

    # Verify
    target_count = spark.table(target_table).count()
    print(f"Target rows: {target_count:,}")
    assert target_count == source_count, (
        f"Row count mismatch: source={source_count}, target={target_count}"
    )
    print(f"PASS: {target_table}")

    return {
        "source": source_table,
        "target": target_table,
        "rows": target_count,
        "status": "OK",
    }

# COMMAND ----------

# Run ingestion for all tables
results = []
for table_config in TABLES:
    try:
        result = ingest_table(table_config)
        results.append(result)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"FAIL: {table_config['source']} — {e}")
        results.append({
            "source": table_config["source"],
            "target": table_config["target"],
            "rows": 0,
            "status": f"ERROR: {e}",
        })

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Ingestion Summary

# COMMAND ----------

import pandas as pd

summary_df = spark.createDataFrame(pd.DataFrame(results))
display(summary_df)

# Print totals
total_rows = sum(r["rows"] for r in results)
ok_count = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == "OK")
fail_count = len(results) - ok_count
print(f"\nTotal: {total_rows:,} rows across {len(results)} tables")
print(f"OK: {ok_count} | FAILED: {fail_count}")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Verify Bronze Tables

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC

pandas as pd summary_df = spark.createDataFrame(pd.DataFrame(results)) display(summary_df) # Print totals total_rows = sum(r["rows"] for r in results) ok_count = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == "OK") fail_count = len(results) - ok_count print(f"\nTotal: {total_rows:,} rows across {len(results)} tables") print(f"OK: {ok_count} | FAILED: {fail_count}") # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC ## Verify Bronze Tables # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %sql # MAGIC-- List all bronze tables
# MAGIC SHOW TABLES IN ipai.bronze;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Sample: res_partner
# MAGIC SELECT id, name, email, is_company, active, _bronze_ingested_at
# MAGIC FROM ipai.bronze.odoo_res_partner
# MAGIC LIMIT 20;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Sample: account_move
# MAGIC SELECT id, name, move_type, state, amount_total, date, _bronze_ingested_at
# MAGIC FROM ipai.bronze.odoo_account_move
# MAGIC LIMIT 20;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC -- Sample: project_task
# MAGIC SELECT id, name, stage_id, project_id, date_deadline, _bronze_ingested_at
# MAGIC FROM ipai.bronze.odoo_project_task
# MAGIC LIMIT 20;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Next Steps
# MAGIC
# MAGIC 1. **Silver layer**: Deduplicate, normalize, and type-cast in `02_odoo_silver_transform.py`
# MAGIC 2. **Incremental**: Switch from full-load to `write_date`-based incremental using `MERGE INTO`
# MAGIC 3. **Scheduling**: Register as a Databricks Job via DAB (`databricks/bundles/`)
# MAGIC 4. **Monitoring**: Add row-count assertions + data-quality checks (Great Expectations or DLT expectations)

# Databricks notebook source
# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC # Diva Goals Evidence Pipeline
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Orchestrates the evidence normalization and review mart computation
# MAGIC for the Diva Goals product family.
# MAGIC
# MAGIC **Contract**: `contracts/diva_goals_evidence.yaml`
# MAGIC **Silver output**: `ipai_lakehouse.silver.diva_evidence_normalized`
# MAGIC **Gold output**: `ipai_lakehouse.gold.diva_review_mart`
# MAGIC
# MAGIC **Trigger**: Scheduled daily or on-demand after goal-status-synthesis workflow.

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Configuration

# COMMAND ----------

CATALOG = "ipai_lakehouse"
SILVER_SCHEMA = "silver"
GOLD_SCHEMA = "gold"

# Evidence freshness thresholds (days)
GOAL_STALE_DAYS = 30
TASK_STALE_DAYS = 14
REVIEW_OVERDUE_DAYS = 7

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Pipeline Execution
# MAGIC
# MAGIC This notebook is a DLT pipeline orchestrator. The actual transformations
# MAGIC are defined in:
# MAGIC - `lakehouse/silver/diva_evidence_normalized.py`
# MAGIC - `lakehouse/gold/diva_review_mart.py`
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Run this notebook to trigger the pipeline, or configure it as a
# MAGIC Databricks job with a daily schedule.

# COMMAND ----------

# Validate pipeline outputs
spark.sql(f"""
    SELECT
        COUNT(*) as total_goals,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'on_track' THEN 1 END) as on_track,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'at_risk' THEN 1 END) as at_risk,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'blocked' THEN 1 END) as blocked,
        AVG(confidence) as avg_confidence,
        AVG(evidence_freshness_pct) as avg_freshness
    FROM {CATALOG}.{GOLD_SCHEMA}.diva_review_mart
""").display()

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Quality Checks

# COMMAND ----------

# Check for goals without evidence
orphan_check = spark.sql(f"""
    SELECT goal_id, goal_name, evidence_count
    FROM {CATALOG}.{GOLD_SCHEMA}.diva_review_mart
    WHERE evidence_count = 0
""")

if orphan_check.count() > 0:
    print(f"WARNING: {orphan_check.count()} goals have zero evidence items")
    orphan_check.display()
else:
    print("OK: All goals have at least one evidence item")

# COMMAND ----------

# Check evidence freshness
freshness_check = spark.sql(f"""
    SELECT
        goal_id, goal_name, evidence_freshness_pct,
        stale_evidence_count, fresh_evidence_count
    FROM {CATALOG}.{GOLD_SCHEMA}.diva_review_mart
    WHERE evidence_freshness_pct < 0.50
""")

if freshness_check.count() > 0:
    print(f"WARNING: {freshness_check.count()} goals have less than 50% fresh evidence")
    freshness_check.display()
else:
    print("OK: All goals have >= 50% fresh evidence")

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC # Cleanup Test Data
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Drops all test schemas created by the synthetic data harness.
# MAGIC
# MAGIC **Schemas dropped:**
# MAGIC - `dbw_ipai_dev.test_bronze`
# MAGIC - `dbw_ipai_dev.test_silver`
# MAGIC - `dbw_ipai_dev.test_gold`
# MAGIC
# MAGIC **Safety**: Only drops schemas prefixed with `test_` in the dev catalog.
# MAGIC Will NOT touch production bronze/silver/gold schemas.

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Pre-Cleanup Inventory

# COMMAND ----------

catalog = "dbw_ipai_dev"
test_schemas = ["test_bronze", "test_silver", "test_gold"]

for schema in test_schemas:
    fqn = f"{catalog}.{schema}"
    try:
        tables = spark.sql(f"SHOW TABLES IN {fqn}").collect()
        print(f"{fqn}: {len(tables)} tables")
        for t in tables:
            row_count = spark.table(f"{fqn}.{t.tableName}").count()
            print(f"  - {t.tableName}: {row_count:,} rows")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{fqn}: does not exist or inaccessible ({e})")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Drop Test Schemas
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Using CASCADE to drop all tables within each schema.

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_bronze CASCADE;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_silver CASCADE;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_gold CASCADE;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Verify Cleanup

# COMMAND ----------

for schema in test_schemas:
    fqn = f"{catalog}.{schema}"
    try:
        spark.sql(f"SHOW TABLES IN {fqn}").collect()
        print(f"WARNING: {fqn} still exists!")
    except Exception:
        print(f"CONFIRMED: {fqn} dropped successfully")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Done
# MAGIC
# MAGIC All test schemas have been removed. Production schemas are untouched.

# COMMAND ----------

dbutils.notebook.exit("cleanup_complete")

# MAGIC into `dbw_ipai_dev.test_bronze.*` for medallion pipeline stress testing.

# MAGIC - `seed`: random seed for reproducibility (default: 42)

# MAGIC - Schema: `test_bronze`

dbutils.widgets.text("scale_profile", "small", "Scale Profile")

# MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_bronze
# MAGIC   COMMENT 'Synthetic bronze layer for medallion pipeline testing';
# MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_silver

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Generate Synthetic Data

# COMMAND ----------

import sys
import time
from datetime import datetime

# Add the repo root to path so we can import the generator
# Adjust if repo is mounted differently in your workspace
sys.path.insert(0, "/Workspace/Repos/data-intelligence")

from tests.synthetic.data_generator import generate_all

start_ts = time.time()
dataset = generate_all(profile_name=scale_profile, seed=seed)
gen_duration = round(time.time() - start_ts, 2)

summary = dataset.summary()
print(f"Generation complete in {gen_duration}s")
for table, count in summary.items():
    print(f"  {table}: {count:,} rows")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC

Convert to Spark DataFrames and Write to Delta

# COMMAND ----------

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.types import (
    BooleanType,
    DoubleType,
    IntegerType,
    StringType,
    StructField,
    StructType,
    TimestampType,
)

spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

CATALOG = "dbw_ipai_dev"
SCHEMA = "test_bronze"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schema definitions (canonical — drift columns are handled via mergeSchema)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

customers_schema = StructType([
    StructField("customer_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("name", StringType(), False),
    StructField("email", StringType(), True),
    StructField("phone", StringType(), True),
    StructField("region", StringType(), True),
    StructField("currency", StringType(), True),
    StructField("is_active", BooleanType(), True),
    StructField("created_at", StringType(), True),
])

invoices_schema = StructType([
    StructField("invoice_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("customer_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("invoice_number", StringType(), True),
    StructField("invoice_date", StringType(), True),
    StructField("due_date", StringType(), True),
    StructField("currency", StringType(), True),
    StructField("status", StringType(), True),
    StructField("total_amount", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("ingest_ts", StringType(), True),
])

line_items_schema = StructType([
    StructField("line_item_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("invoice_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("sequence", IntegerType(), True),
    StructField("product_name", StringType(), True),
    StructField("quantity", IntegerType(), True),
    StructField("unit_price", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("discount_pct", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("line_total", DoubleType(), True),
])

payments_schema = StructType([
    StructField("payment_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("invoice_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("payment_date", StringType(), True),
    StructField("amount", DoubleType(), True),
    StructField("method", StringType(), True),
    StructField("reference", StringType(), True),
])

events_schema = StructType([
    StructField("event_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("customer_id", StringType(), False),
    StructField("event_type", StringType(), True),
    StructField("event_ts", StringType(), True),
    StructField("ingest_ts", StringType(), True),
    StructField("payload", StringType(), True),
])

table_configs = {
    "customers": (dataset.customers, customers_schema),
    "invoices": (dataset.invoices, invoices_schema),
    "line_items": (dataset.line_items, line_items_schema),
    "payments": (dataset.payments, payments_schema),
    "events": (dataset.events, events_schema),
}

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC

StringType(), False), StructField("payment_date", StringType(), True), StructField("amount", DoubleType(), True), StructField("method", StringType(), True), StructField("reference", StringType(), True), ]) events_schema = StructType([ StructField("event_id", StringType(), False), StructField("customer_id", StringType(), False), StructField("event_type", StringType(), True), StructField("event_ts", StringType(), True), StructField("ingest_ts", StringType(), True), StructField("payload", StringType(), True), ]) table_configs = { "customers": (dataset.customers, customers_schema), "invoices": (dataset.invoices, invoices_schema), "line_items": (dataset.line_items, line_items_schema), "payments": (dataset.payments, payments_schema), "events": (dataset.events, events_schema), } # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC## Write Tables

# COMMAND ----------

ingestion_metrics = {}

for table_name, (rows, schema) in table_configs.items():
    t0 = time.time()
    target = f"{CATALOG}.{SCHEMA}.{table_name}"

    # Create DataFrame — use mergeSchema to handle drift columns
    df = spark.createDataFrame(rows, schema=schema) if not any(
        k.startswith("_drift_") for row in rows for k in row
    ) else spark.createDataFrame(rows)

    # Overwrite to ensure idempotent seeding
    (
        df.write
        .format("delta")
        .mode("overwrite")
        .option("overwriteSchema", "true")
        .option("mergeSchema", "true")
        .saveAsTable(target)
    )

    write_duration = round(time.time() - t0, 2)
    row_count = spark.table(target).count()

    ingestion_metrics[table_name] = {
        "rows_written": row_count,
        "write_duration_s": write_duration,
        "target": target,
    }

    print(f"  {target}: {row_count:,} rows in {write_duration}s")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Ingestion Summary

# COMMAND ----------

import json

result = {
    "profile": scale_profile,
    "seed": seed,
    "generation_duration_s": gen_duration,
    "generation_ts": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    "tables": ingestion_metrics,
    "total_rows": sum(m["rows_written"] for m in ingestion_metrics.values()),
}

print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

# Store as notebook output for downstream consumption
dbutils.notebook.exit(json.dumps(result))

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC # Medallion Pipeline Test — Bronze → Silver → Gold
# MAGIC
# MAGIC Runs the full medallion transformation on synthetic test data in
# MAGIC `dbw_ipai_dev.test_bronze.*`, producing `test_silver.*` and `test_gold.*`.
# MAGIC
# MAGIC ## Transforms
# MAGIC - **Bronze → Silver**: Dedup, type casting, null quarantine, timestamp normalization
# MAGIC - **Silver → Gold**: Revenue summary, customer KPIs, payment aging
# MAGIC
# MAGIC ## Prerequisites
# MAGIC - Run `seed_synthetic` notebook first to populate `test_bronze.*`

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC ## Configuration

# COMMAND ----------

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, DataFrame
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
from pyspark.sql.window import Window
import time
import json
from datetime import datetime

spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()

CATALOG = "dbw_ipai_dev"
BRONZE = f"{CATALOG}.test_bronze"
SILVER = f"{CATALOG}.test_silver"
GOLD = f"{CATALOG}.test_gold"

metrics = {
    "run_ts": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    "bronze_to_silver": {},
    "silver_to_gold": {},
    "quality": {},
}

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ## Ensure Silver and Gold Schemas Exist

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %sql
# MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_silver;
# MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_gold;

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ---
# MAGIC # Stage 1: Bronze → Silver

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC #

} # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC ## Ensure Silver and Gold Schemas Exist # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %sql # MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_silver; # MAGIC CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS dbw_ipai_dev.test_gold; # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC --- # MAGIC # Stage 1: Bronze → Silver # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC ### 1.1 Customers: Dedup + Type Enforcement

# COMMAND ----------

t0 = time.time()

bronze_customers = spark.table(f"{BRONZE}.customers")
bronze_count = bronze_customers.count()

# Dedup on customer_id — keep latest by created_at
window_cust = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(F.col("created_at").desc())
silver_customers = (
    bronze_customers
    .withColumn("_row_num", F.row_number().over(window_cust))
    .filter(F.col("_row_num") == 1)
    .drop("_row_num")
    # Drop schema drift columns
    .select([c for c in bronze_customers.columns if not c.startswith("_drift_")])
    # Type enforcement
    .withColumn("created_at", F.to_timestamp("created_at"))
    .withColumn("is_active", F.col("is_active").cast("boolean"))
)

# Quarantine: rows missing customer_id or name
quarantine_customers = silver_customers.filter(
    F.col("customer_id").isNull() | F.col("name").isNull()
)
silver_customers_clean = silver_customers.filter(
    F.col("customer_id").isNotNull() & F.col("name").isNotNull()
)

silver_customers_clean.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{SILVER}.customers")
if quarantine_customers.count() > 0:
    quarantine_customers.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{SILVER}.customers_quarantine")

silver_count = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.customers").count()
quarantine_count = quarantine_customers.count()
dup_removed = bronze_count - silver_count - quarantine_count

metrics["bronze_to_silver"]["customers"] = {
    "bronze_rows": bronze_count,
    "silver_rows": silver_count,
    "duplicates_removed": dup_removed,
    "quarantine_rows": quarantine_count,
    "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
}
print(f"Customers: {bronze_count} → {silver_count} (dedup: {dup_removed}, quarantine: {quarantine_count})")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC #

1.2 Invoices: Dedup + Late-Arriving Normalization

# COMMAND ----------

t0 = time.time()

bronze_invoices = spark.table(f"{BRONZE}.invoices")
bronze_count = bronze_invoices.count()

window_inv = Window.partitionBy("invoice_id").orderBy(F.col("ingest_ts").desc())
silver_invoices = (
    bronze_invoices
    .withColumn("_row_num", F.row_number().over(window_inv))
    .filter(F.col("_row_num") == 1)
    .drop("_row_num")
    .select([c for c in bronze_invoices.columns if not c.startswith("_drift_")])
    .withColumn("invoice_date", F.to_date("invoice_date"))
    .withColumn("due_date", F.to_date("due_date"))
    .withColumn("ingest_ts", F.to_timestamp("ingest_ts"))
    .withColumn("total_amount", F.col("total_amount").cast("double"))
    # Flag late-arriving records (ingest > event + 24h)
    .withColumn(
        "is_late_arriving",
        F.when(
            F.unix_timestamp("ingest_ts") - F.unix_timestamp(F.col("invoice_date").cast("timestamp")) > 86400,
            F.lit(True),
        ).otherwise(F.lit(False)),
    )
)

quarantine_invoices = silver_invoices.filter(
    F.col("invoice_id").isNull() | F.col("customer_id").isNull()
)
silver_invoices_clean = silver_invoices.filter(
    F.col("invoice_id").isNotNull() & F.col("customer_id").isNotNull()
)

silver_invoices_clean.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{SILVER}.invoices")
if quarantine_invoices.count() > 0:
    quarantine_invoices.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{SILVER}.invoices_quarantine")

silver_count = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.invoices").count()
quarantine_count = quarantine_invoices.count()
late_count = silver_invoices_clean.filter(F.col("is_late_arriving")).count()

metrics["bronze_to_silver"]["invoices"] = {
    "bronze_rows": bronze_count,
    "silver_rows": silver_count,
    "duplicates_removed": bronze_count - silver_count - quarantine_count,
    "quarantine_rows": quarantine_count,
    "late_arriving_rows": late_count,
    "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
}
print(f"Invoices: {bronze_count} → {silver_count} (late: {late_count}, quarantine: {quarantine_count})")

# COMMAND ----------
