.withColumn( "is_late_arriving", F.when( F.unix_timestamp("ingest_ts") - F.unix_timestamp("event_ts") > 86400, F.lit(True), ).otherwise(F.lit(False)), ) ) silver_ev.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{SILVER}.events") silver_count = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.events").count() metrics["bronze_to_silver"]["events"] = { "bronze_rows": bronze_count, "silver_rows": silver_count, "duplicates_removed": bronze_count - silver_count, "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2), } print(f"Events: {bronze_count} → {silver_count}") # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC --- # MAGIC # Stage 2: Silver → Gold # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC ### 2.1 Gold: Revenue Summary (monthly, by currency)

# COMMAND ----------

t0 = time.time()

silver_inv = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.invoices")
silver_li = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.line_items")

revenue_summary = (
    silver_inv
    .filter(F.col("status").isin("posted", "paid"))
    .join(silver_li, "invoice_id", "inner")
    .withColumn("year_month", F.date_format("invoice_date", "yyyy-MM"))
    .groupBy("year_month", "currency")
    .agg(
        F.count("invoice_id").alias("invoice_count"),
        F.sum("line_total").alias("total_revenue"),
        F.avg("line_total").alias("avg_line_amount"),
        F.countDistinct("customer_id").alias("unique_customers"),
    )
    .orderBy("year_month", "currency")
)

revenue_summary.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{GOLD}.revenue_summary")
gold_rev_count = spark.table(f"{GOLD}.revenue_summary").count()

metrics["silver_to_gold"]["revenue_summary"] = {
    "gold_rows": gold_rev_count,
    "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
}
print(f"Revenue Summary: {gold_rev_count} rows")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC #

2.2 Gold: Customer KPIs

# COMMAND ----------

t0 = time.time()

silver_cust = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.customers")
silver_inv = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.invoices")
silver_pay = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.payments")
silver_ev = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.events")

# Invoice metrics per customer
inv_metrics = (
    silver_inv
    .groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        F.count("invoice_id").alias("total_invoices"),
        F.sum("total_amount").alias("lifetime_revenue"),
        F.min("invoice_date").alias("first_invoice_date"),
        F.max("invoice_date").alias("last_invoice_date"),
    )
)

# Payment metrics per customer
pay_metrics = (
    silver_pay
    .join(silver_inv.select("invoice_id", "customer_id"), "invoice_id", "inner")
    .groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        F.count("payment_id").alias("total_payments"),
        F.sum("amount").alias("total_paid"),
    )
)

# Event metrics per customer
ev_metrics = (
    silver_ev
    .groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(
        F.count("event_id").alias("total_events"),
        F.max("event_ts").alias("last_activity"),
    )
)

customer_kpis = (
    silver_cust
    .join(inv_metrics, "customer_id", "left")
    .join(pay_metrics, "customer_id", "left")
    .join(ev_metrics, "customer_id", "left")
    .withColumn("outstanding_amount",
        F.coalesce(F.col("lifetime_revenue"), F.lit(0)) - F.coalesce(F.col("total_paid"), F.lit(0))
    )
    .withColumn("payment_coverage_pct",
        F.when(F.col("lifetime_revenue") > 0,
            F.round(F.coalesce(F.col("total_paid"), F.lit(0)) / F.col("lifetime_revenue") * 100, 2)
        ).otherwise(F.lit(0))
    )
    .select(
        "customer_id", "name", "region", "currency", "is_active",
        "total_invoices", "lifetime_revenue", "first_invoice_date", "last_invoice_date",
        "total_payments", "total_paid", "outstanding_amount", "payment_coverage_pct",
        "total_events", "last_activity",
    )
)

customer_kpis.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{GOLD}.customer_kpis")
gold_kpi_count = spark.table(f"{GOLD}.customer_kpis").count()

metrics["silver_to_gold"]["customer_kpis"] = {
    "gold_rows": gold_kpi_count,
    "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
}
print(f"Customer KPIs: {gold_kpi_count} rows")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC #

2.3 Gold: Payment Aging

# COMMAND ----------

t0 = time.time()

silver_inv = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.invoices")
silver_pay = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.payments")

# For each invoice, compute days between invoice_date and payment_date
payment_aging = (
    silver_inv
    .filter(F.col("status").isin("posted", "paid"))
    .join(silver_pay, "invoice_id", "left")
    .withColumn(
        "days_to_payment",
        F.when(
            F.col("payment_date").isNotNull(),
            F.datediff(F.col("payment_date"), F.col("invoice_date")),
        ),
    )
    .withColumn(
        "aging_bucket",
        F.when(F.col("days_to_payment").isNull(), "unpaid")
        .when(F.col("days_to_payment") <= 0, "prepaid")
        .when(F.col("days_to_payment") <= 15, "0-15 days")
        .when(F.col("days_to_payment") <= 30, "16-30 days")
        .when(F.col("days_to_payment") <= 60, "31-60 days")
        .when(F.col("days_to_payment") <= 90, "61-90 days")
        .otherwise("90+ days"),
    )
    .groupBy("aging_bucket", "currency")
    .agg(
        F.count("invoice_id").alias("invoice_count"),
        F.sum("total_amount").alias("total_amount"),
        F.avg("days_to_payment").alias("avg_days_to_payment"),
    )
    .orderBy("aging_bucket", "currency")
)

payment_aging.write.format("delta").mode("overwrite").option("overwriteSchema", "true").saveAsTable(f"{GOLD}.payment_aging")
gold_aging_count = spark.table(f"{GOLD}.payment_aging").count()

metrics["silver_to_gold"]["payment_aging"] = {
    "gold_rows": gold_aging_count,
    "duration_s": round(time.time() - t0, 2),
}
print(f"Payment Aging: {gold_aging_count} rows")

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md
# MAGIC ---
# MAGIC # Quality Metrics Summary

# COMMAND ----------

# Compute aggregate quality metrics
total_bronze = sum(m.get("bronze_rows", 0) for m in metrics["bronze_to_silver"].values())
total_silver = sum(m.get("silver_rows", 0) for m in metrics["bronze_to_silver"].values())
total_dups = sum(m.get("duplicates_removed", 0) for m in metrics["bronze_to_silver"].values())
total_quarantine = sum(m.get("quarantine_rows", 0) for m in metrics["bronze_to_silver"].values())

# Null rates in Silver
null_rates = {}
for table_name in ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]:
    df = spark.table(f"{SILVER}.{table_name}")
    total = df.count()
    if total == 0:
        continue
    nullable_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in ("customer_id", "invoice_id", "line_item_id", "payment_id", "event_id")]
    null_counts = {}
    for col in nullable_cols:
        nc = df.filter(F.col(col).isNull()).count()
        if nc > 0:
            null_counts[col] = round(nc / total, 4)
    null_rates[table_name] = null_counts

metrics["quality"] = {
    "total_bronze_rows": total_bronze,
    "total_silver_rows": total_silver,
    "total_duplicates_removed": total_dups,
    "total_quarantine_rows": total_quarantine,
    "dedup_rate_pct": round(total_dups / total_bronze * 100, 2) if total_bronze else 0,
    "quarantine_rate_pct": round(total_quarantine / total_bronze * 100, 2) if total_bronze else 0,
    "null_rates_by_table": null_rates,
}

print(json.dumps(metrics, indent=2, default=str))

# COMMAND ----------

# MAGIC %md

nullable_cols: nc = df.filter(F.col(col).isNull()).count() if nc > 0: null_counts[col] = round(nc / total, 4) null_rates[table_name] = null_counts metrics["quality"] = { "total_bronze_rows": total_bronze, "total_silver_rows": total_silver, "total_duplicates_removed": total_dups, "total_quarantine_rows": total_quarantine, "dedup_rate_pct": round(total_dups / total_bronze * 100, 2) if total_bronze else 0, "quarantine_rate_pct": round(total_quarantine / total_bronze * 100, 2) if total_bronze else 0, "null_rates_by_table": null_rates, } print(json.dumps(metrics, indent=2, default=str)) # COMMAND ---------- # MAGIC %md # MAGIC## Exit with Results

# Bronze Layer Schemas

Raw ingestion contracts. These schemas define the expected structure of data as it arrives from source systems before any transformation.

## Convention

- One schema file per source system
- Schema format: YAML with JSON Schema validation rules
- File naming: `<source_system>_<entity>.yaml`

<!-- TODO: Add first bronze schema when Fabric mirroring from Odoo PG is active -->

# Gold Layer Schemas

Business-ready aggregate schemas. Gold layer produces pre-computed metrics, dimensions, and fact tables for BI and API consumption.

## Convention

- One schema file per business domain aggregate
- Schema format: YAML with JSON Schema validation rules
- File naming: `<domain>_<aggregate>.yaml`
- Must reference upstream silver schemas

<!-- TODO: Add first gold schema when Power BI semantic model is defined -->

# Silver Layer Schemas

Cleansed and conformed schemas. Silver layer applies deduplication, type casting, null handling, and cross-source key alignment.

## Convention

- One schema file per conformed entity
- Schema format: YAML with JSON Schema validation rules
- File naming: `<domain>_<entity>.yaml`

<!-- TODO: Add first silver schema when DLT pipelines produce conformed output -->

# Data Governance Spec Bundle

Specification bundle for data governance: Unity Catalog policies, access controls, data classification, lineage tracking, and audit requirements.

## Contents

<!-- TODO: Add spec files as governance policies are codified -->
- `constitution.md` -- Design principles and constraints
- `plan.md` -- Implementation plan
- `prd.md` -- Product requirements
- `tasks.md` -- Task breakdown

Specification bundle for the lakehouse architecture: medallion layers, Unity Catalog governance, Fabric mirroring, and data quality contracts.

version: 1
namespace: lakehouse.knowledge.databricks_apps_lakebase
last_reviewed: "2026-03-15"
source:
  kind: vendor_guide_extract
  title: "A Hands-On Guide to Apps on Databricks"
  vendor: Databricks
  year: 2026
description: >
  Knowledge base extracted from the Databricks hands-on guide covering
  Databricks Apps, Lakebase, DABs, reverse ETL, secure real-time apps,
  and AI agents with conversational memory. This is the reference for
  how Databricks positions its application platform layer and what
  patterns Odoo Copilot should benchmark against for analytics/intelligence.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. Platform Components
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
platform_components:
  databricks_apps:
    label: Databricks Apps
    type: serverless_app_hosting
    description: >
      Serverless application hosting platform for web applications built
      with open-source frameworks (Streamlit, Dash, Flask, FastAPI, React).
      No infrastructure to provision, no containers to manage, no scaling
      policies to configure.
    key_capabilities:
      - Serverless compute with zero infrastructure management
      - Built-in OAuth and Unity Catalog integration
      - Service principal authentication for app-level access
      - On-behalf-of-user authorization for user-level permissions
      - Static file serving via FastAPI StaticFiles
      - Automatic environment variable injection (PGHOST, PGUSER, DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID)
    supported_frameworks:
      - Streamlit
      - Dash
      - Flask
      - FastAPI
      - React
      - Gradio
      - Plotly Dash
      - Shiny
    auth_modes:
      - app_authorization: Service principal identity
      - user_authorization: On-behalf-of-user (OBO)

management - Built-in OAuth and Unity Catalog integration - Service principal authentication for app-level access - On-behalf-of-user authorization for user-level permissions - Static file serving via FastAPI StaticFiles - Automatic environment variable injection (PGHOST, PGUSER, DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID) supported_frameworks: - Streamlit - Dash - Flask - FastAPI - React - Gradio - Plotly Dash - Shiny auth_modes: - app_authorization: Service principal identity - user_authorization: On-behalf-of-user (OBO)

  lakebase:
    label: Lakebase
    type: managed_postgres
    description: >
      Fully managed PostgreSQL database deeply integrated with the lakehouse.
      Provides transactional, low-latency data access with automatic
      synchronization from Unity Catalog tables. Postgres-compatible with
      support for ACID transactions, indexes, joins, and extensions.
    key_capabilities:
      - Sub-10ms query latency
      - Thousands of QPS
      - Multi-AZ high availability
      - Point-in-time recovery
      - Encrypted storage
      - Compute/storage separation for independent scaling
      - Database branching
      - OAuth M2M authentication
      - Unity Catalog governance integration
      - Cloud-agnostic architecture
    extensions:
      - PostGIS
      - pgvector
      - pg_stat_statements
      - databricks_auth
    sync_modes:
      snapshot:
        description: Full table replacement on each run
        latency: high
        cost: lowest
        best_for:
          - Infrequent changes
          - Modifying >10% of source table
          - Low-urgency high-volume updates
        limitations:
          - Full table re-creation each time
      triggered:
        description: Initial full copy then incremental changes on demand
        latency: medium
        cost: balanced
        best_for:
          - Compromise between cost and latency
          - Reasonably current data
          - Controlled refresh timing
        limitations:
          - Requires change data feed
          - Avoid running more frequently than every 5 minutes
      continuous:
        description: Initial load then real-time streaming updates (~15s)
        latency: lowest
        cost: highest
        best_for:
          - Mission-critical systems
          - Real-time data requirements
          - No manual refresh tolerance
        limitations:
          - Requires change data feed
          - Continuous resource consumption

Compromise between cost and latency - Reasonably current data - Controlled refresh timing limitations: - Requires change data feed - Avoid running more frequently than every 5 minutes continuous: description: Initial load then real-time streaming updates (~15s) latency: lowest cost: highest best_for: - Mission-critical systems - Real-time data requirements - No manual refresh tolerance limitations: - Requires change data feed - Continuous resource consumption

  databricks_asset_bundles:
    label: Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs)
    type: deployment_framework
    description: >
      Declarative deployment framework that packages application code,
      infrastructure definitions, and data sync configurations into a
      single version-controlled bundle. Deploys consistently across
      dev, staging, and production environments.
    key_capabilities:
      - Single-command deployment
      - Environment-specific configurations via variables
      - CI/CD integration (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps)
      - Resource definitions for apps, database instances, synced tables
      - Modular and reusable bundle patterns

  unity_catalog:
    label: Unity Catalog
    type: governance
    description: >
      Unified governance layer for data, AI, and application resources.
      Provides fine-grained access control, audit trails, lineage tracking,
      and consistent policy enforcement across analytical and operational systems.

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. Architecture Patterns
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
architecture_patterns:
  notebook_to_production:
    label: From Notebooks to Production Applications
    description: >
      Pattern for moving from analytical notebooks to deployed production
      apps using Databricks Apps + Lakebase + DABs.
    components:
      - React/TypeScript/Vite frontend
      - FastAPI backend
      - Lakebase synced table (read-only, auto-synced from Unity Catalog)
      - DABs bundle for deployment
    data_flow:
      - Unity Catalog Delta table is source of truth
      - Synced table mirrors Delta table to Postgres automatically
      - App queries Postgres via OAuth-authenticated connection
      - Changes in source Delta table appear in app within seconds
    auth_pattern:
      - App service principal obtains OAuth token via WorkspaceClient
      - Token auto-refreshes (1-hour expiry)
      - Psycopg adapter connects to Lakebase with token as password

for deployment data_flow: - Unity Catalog Delta table is source of truth - Synced table mirrors Delta table to Postgres automatically - App queries Postgres via OAuth-authenticated connection - Changes in source Delta table appear in app within seconds auth_pattern: - App service principal obtains OAuth token via WorkspaceClient - Token auto-refreshes (1-hour expiry) - Psycopg adapter connects to Lakebase with token as password

  transactional_foundation:
    label: Transactional Foundation for Intelligent Applications
    description: >
      Pattern for building OLTP apps that read AND write to Lakebase,
      with governed data access and built-in identity controls.
    example: Holiday request approval app
    components:
      - Streamlit or Dash frontend
      - SQLAlchemy ORM layer
      - Lakebase database with app-created tables
      - OAuth token management via Databricks SDK
    key_insight: >
      App service principal gets automatic Postgres role creation when
      Lakebase is added as app resource. GRANT statements control
      schema/table-level access.

  reverse_etl:
    label: Turning Analytics Into Applications (Reverse ETL)
    description: >
      Pattern for syncing curated lakehouse data into operational
      applications. Lakebase replaces fragmented reverse ETL setups
      with a fully managed, integrated capability.
    components:
      - Lakehouse Gold tables as source
      - Lakebase synced tables (continuous mode for real-time)
      - Separate state tables for user-written data
      - Flask/Streamlit app for interactive dashboard
    key_insight: >
      Synced tables handle analytical data flow. Separate tables handle
      user state. This separation is critical for clean architecture.
    example: Intelligent support portal with ML-driven incident triage

  secure_realtime:
    label: Delivering Secure Real-Time Applications
    description: >
      Pattern for CI/CD-enabled apps with OAuth security, DABs deployment,
      and synced Lakebase tables.
    components:
      - Streamlit UI
      - SQLAlchemy + Databricks SDK for secure connection
      - DABs for declarative deployment
      - GitHub for version control and pipeline automation
    auth_details:
      token_refresh: Every 15 minutes
      method: OAuth via Databricks SDK
      no_manual_token_management: true

support portal with ML-driven incident triage secure_realtime: label: Delivering Secure Real-Time Applications description: > Pattern for CI/CD-enabled apps with OAuth security, DABs deployment, and synced Lakebase tables. components: - Streamlit UI - SQLAlchemy + Databricks SDK for secure connection - DABs for declarative deployment - GitHub for version control and pipeline automation auth_details: token_refresh: Every 15 minutes method: OAuth via Databricks SDK no_manual_token_management: true

  stateful_ai_agents:
    label: AI Agents With Conversational Memory
    description: >
      Pattern for building stateful AI agents that persist conversational
      memory across sessions using Lakebase as the checkpoint store for
      LangGraph.
    components:
      - Chat model served on Databricks
      - Unity Catalog functions as governed tools
      - LangGraph orchestration for control flow
      - Lakebase as transactional checkpointer
      - Databricks AI Agent Framework for deployment
      - Streamlit Databricks app for end-user interaction
      - MLflow for tracing and lifecycle management
    memory_patterns:
      short_term:
        description: Thread-level checkpointing for conversation continuity
        storage: Lakebase via langgraph-checkpoint-postgres
        identifier: thread_id
        capability: Pause and resume investigations across sessions
      long_term:
        description: Persists user preferences and accumulated findings across sessions
        storage: Lakebase
        considerations:
          - Scope management
          - Retention policies
          - Governance controls
    key_libraries:
      - databricks-langchain
      - unitycatalog-langchain
      - langgraph-checkpoint-postgres==2.0.21
      - langgraph==0.3.4
      - mlflow
    deployment:
      framework: Databricks AI Agent Framework
      steps:
        - Log agent with MLflow (models from code approach)
        - Declare dependent resources (endpoints, functions, Lakebase)
        - Deploy with agents.deploy()
        - Test via AI Playground or Review App
        - Expose via Streamlit Databricks app

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. Code Patterns (Reference)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
code_patterns:
  lakebase_oauth_connection:
    language: python
    description: OAuth-authenticated Psycopg connection to Lakebase
    key_elements:
      - WorkspaceClient handles token refresh
      - Token used as password in psycopg.connect()
      - sslmode=require always
      - PGHOST and database from environment variables

  sqlalchemy_oauth_connection:
    language: python
    description: SQLAlchemy engine with OAuth token injection
    key_elements:
      - create_engine with postgresql+psycopg driver
      - event.listens_for(pool, "do_connect") hook for token injection
      - Token refresh every 15 minutes via WorkspaceClient
      - client_id as Postgres username

OAuth-authenticated Psycopg connection to Lakebase key_elements: - WorkspaceClient handles token refresh - Token used as password in psycopg.connect() - sslmode=require always - PGHOST and database from environment variables sqlalchemy_oauth_connection: language: python description: SQLAlchemy engine with OAuth token injection key_elements: - create_engine with postgresql+psycopg driver - event.listens_for(pool, "do_connect") hook for token injection - Token refresh every 15 minutes via WorkspaceClient - client_id as Postgres username

  dabs_bundle_structure:
    description: Minimal DABs bundle for app + Lakebase
    files:
      - databricks.yml (bundle config, targets, variables)
      - resources/database.yml (database_instance + synced_database_table)
      - resources/app.yml (app resource with Lakebase reference)
      - app/ (application source code)
      - requirements.txt (Python dependencies)

  langgraph_checkpoint:
    language: python
    description: LangGraph checkpointing with Lakebase
    key_elements:
      - CheckpointSaver(instance_name=LAKEBASE_INSTANCE_NAME) as context manager
      - graph.stream() with checkpoint_config containing thread_id
      - DatabricksLakebase resource declaration for MLflow logging
      - predict_stream pattern for streaming responses

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. Best Practices (from guide)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
best_practices:
  deployment:
    - Use DABs for single-command, reproducible deployments
    - Define environment-specific configs via DABs variables
    - Automate with CI/CD (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps)
    - Use modular, reusable bundle patterns

  lakebase:
    - Choose sync mode based on cost/latency tradeoff
    - Set up notifications for sync pipeline failures
    - Right-size instance capacity for workload
    - Create indexes matching query patterns
    - Use pg_stat_statements for query performance analysis
    - Separate synced tables (read-only) from app state tables (read-write)

  security:
    - Use OAuth 2.0, never legacy PATs in production
    - Use Databricks CLI for local OAuth U2M authentication
    - Apps use service principal for OAuth M2M
    - Use on-behalf-of-user authorization for user-level permissions
    - Store secrets in Databricks secret scopes

  performance:
    - Consider WebSockets or server-sent events instead of polling
    - Implement caching (e.g., fastapi-cache) for in-memory query results
    - Use database branching for safe schema migrations
    - Use Flyway or Liquibase for schema version management

CLI for local OAuth U2M authentication - Apps use service principal for OAuth M2M - Use on-behalf-of-user authorization for user-level permissions - Store secrets in Databricks secret scopes performance: - Consider WebSockets or server-sent events instead of polling - Implement caching (e.g., fastapi-cache) for in-memory query results - Use database branching for safe schema migrations - Use Flyway or Liquibase for schema version management

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# 5. Relevance to Odoo Copilot Benchmark
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────
benchmark_relevance:
  category: analytics_recommendations
  weight: 5
  what_databricks_demonstrates:
    - Notebook-to-production pipeline with zero infrastructure management
    - Managed reverse ETL that eliminates custom sync pipelines
    - Stateful AI agents with persistent conversational memory
    - Unified governance across analytical and operational data
    - SDK-first deployment with DABs
    - OAuth-based security without manual token management
  lessons_for_odoo_copilot:
    - Odoo Copilot should integrate with Databricks for analytics/intelligence rather than replicate it
    - Agent memory patterns (thread-based checkpointing) are directly applicable to Odoo workflows
    - Reverse ETL from lakehouse to operational apps is a solved problem on Databricks
    - Unity Catalog functions as governed tools is the pattern for exposing Odoo data to agents
    - DABs-style declarative deployment is the bar for reproducible agent deployment

version: 2
namespace: lakehouse.benchmark.odoo_copilot
last_reviewed: "2026-03-15"
description: >
  Odoo Copilot Benchmark Scorecard V2 — lakehouse-side reference.
  Canonical source: odoo repo ssot/odoo/odoo_copilot_benchmark_v2.yaml.
  This copy provides the benchmark contract for analytics, dashboards,
  and lakehouse-side evaluation pipelines.

benchmark_anchors:
  - key: odoo_ai
    label: Odoo AI / Odoo AI Agents
    role: Native Odoo embedding, grounded knowledge, in-product actionability
  - key: sap_joule
    label: SAP Joule
    role: Enterprise assistant UX, informational/navigational/transactional support
  - key: sap_afc
    label: SAP Advanced Financial Closing
    role: Close orchestration, task planning, dependency handling, status monitoring
  - key: microsoft_foundry
    label: Microsoft Foundry
    role: >

microsoft_foundry_benchmark_dimensions:
  description: >
    Microsoft Foundry is the bar for agent-platform maturity, not for ERP-native
    workflow depth. Foundry defines the bar for hosted agents, control-plane
    governance, evaluations, monitoring, knowledge connection, and SDK-first
    operationalization of enterprise AI apps and agents.
  dimensions:
    - key: agent_hosting
      label: Agent hosting and orchestration
      foundry_surface: Foundry Agent Service
      odoo_copilot_questions:
        - Can agents be hosted as durable business workers?
        - Can they orchestrate multi-step ERP tasks?
        - Can they persist state safely across sessions/jobs?
    - key: control_plane
      label: Control plane and observability
      foundry_surface: Foundry Control Plane
      odoo_copilot_questions:
        - Prompt/result tracing
        - Action auditability
        - Evaluation harnesses
        - Workflow quality metrics
        - Runtime monitoring
        - Safety/governance controls
    - key: knowledge_integration
      label: Knowledge integration
      foundry_surface: Foundry IQ
      odoo_copilot_questions:
        - Can it connect structured ERP data and document knowledge cleanly?
        - Can it maintain grounded retrieval without brittle custom glue?
        - Can different agents share or isolate knowledge safely?
    - key: sdk_developer_surface
      label: SDK-first developer surface
      foundry_surface: Foundry SDKs (Python, C#, JavaScript, Java)
      odoo_copilot_questions:
        - Is the copilot platform code-first and automatable?
        - Can agents/tools be defined deterministically?
        - Can environments be reproduced in CI/CD?
        - Can evaluation and deployment be scripted end-to-end?
    - key: platform_hardening
      label: Enterprise platform hardening
      foundry_surface: Foundry private endpoints, migration guidance
      odoo_copilot_questions:

        - Production-safe secret handling
        - Lifecycle/migration posture
        - Compatibility discipline as platform primitives evolve
      notes: >
        Microsoft documents migration from the classic portal plus a shift
        toward the Responses API before an August 2026 Assistants API sunset.

# ============================================================
# CRON SCHEDULE — Automated Release Operations
# ============================================================
# Defines all recurring jobs for release governance,
# agent health, and promotion workflows.
# Last updated: 2026-03-15
# ============================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
schedule_id: insightpulseai-cron-schedule

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Daily jobs (every day, business hours)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
daily:
  - id: sync-registries
    name: "Sync release & offer registries"
    cron: "0 7 * * 1-5"
    description: "Pull latest registry state from all sources"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - release-registry
      - offer-registry
      - agent-registry

  - id: refresh-offer-status
    name: "Refresh offer status"
    cron: "15 7 * * 1-5"
    description: "Update publish/block/draft state for all offers"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - offer-registry

  - id: smoke-evals
    name: "Run smoke evals on internal agents"
    cron: "0 8 * * 1-5"
    description: "Lightweight eval pass on all deployed agents"
    owner: ai_team
    targets:
      - odoo-copilot
      - finance-docflow
    alert_on_failure: true

  - id: connector-health
    name: "Check connector health"
    cron: "30 8 * * 1-5"
    description: "Verify all active connections are responsive"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - connection-registry

  - id: status-snapshot
    name: "Publish internal status snapshot"
    cron: "0 9 * * 1-5"
    description: "Generate and push daily ops status to internal dashboard"
    owner: platform_team
    outputs:
      - ops-console-dashboard

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Nightly jobs (off-hours, deeper analysis)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
nightly:
  - id: full-eval-pack
    name: "Run full eval packs on candidate agents"
    cron: "0 1 * * 1-5"
    description: "Complete evaluation suite on agents pending promotion"
    owner: ai_team
    timeout_minutes: 120
    targets:
      - all_candidate_agents
    alert_on_failure: true

  - id: connection-verification
    name: "Refresh connection verification"
    cron: "30 1 * * 1-5"
    description: "Deep connectivity and auth check on all registered connections"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - connection-registry

  - id: policy-drift-detection
    name: "Detect policy drift"
    cron: "0 2 * * 1-5"
    description: "Compare deployed agent configs against policy pack"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - policy-pack
      - agent-registry
    alert_on_failure: true

  - id: release-readiness-score
    name: "Update release readiness score"
    cron: "30 2 * * 1-5"
    description: "Compute readiness score for each release milestone"
    owner: platform_team
    outputs:
      - release-registry
      - ops-console-dashboard

owner: platform_team targets: - connection-registry - id: policy-drift-detection name: "Detect policy drift" cron: "0 2 * * 1-5" description: "Compare deployed agent configs against policy pack" owner: platform_team targets: - policy-pack - agent-registry alert_on_failure: true - id: release-readiness-score name: "Update release readiness score" cron: "30 2 * * 1-5" description: "Compute readiness score for each release milestone" owner: platform_team outputs: - release-registry - ops-console-dashboard

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Weekly jobs (Monday unless noted)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
weekly:
  - id: promote-candidates
    name: "Promote top passing internal candidates"
    cron: "0 10 * * 1"
    description: "Move agents that passed nightly evals to next promotion stage"
    owner: ai_team
    requires_approval: true

  - id: release-notes-draft
    name: "Open release notes draft"
    cron: "0 11 * * 1"
    description: "Auto-generate draft release notes from merged changes"
    owner: platform_team
    outputs:
      - release-notes-draft

  - id: bi-semantic-refresh
    name: "Refresh Power BI / BI semantic export state"
    cron: "0 12 * * 1"
    description: "Update governed metric exports for BI consumption"
    owner: data_team
    targets:
      - semantic-access-layer
      - tableau-connector

  - id: close-stale-blockers
    name: "Close stale blockers"
    cron: "0 14 * * 5"
    description: "Friday — auto-flag or close release blockers older than 14 days"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - release-registry

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Biweekly jobs (every other Monday)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
biweekly:
  - id: staging-release-train
    name: "Staging release train"
    cron: "0 10 * * 1"
    frequency_note: "Runs on odd-numbered weeks only"
    description: "Promote passing candidates from dev to staging"
    owner: platform_team
    requires_approval: true
    gates:
      - all_nightly_evals_pass
      - no_critical_blockers
      - policy_compliance_check

  - id: uat-cycle
    name: "Manual / UAT cycle"
    cron: "0 14 * * 1"
    frequency_note: "Runs on odd-numbered weeks only"
    description: "Trigger UAT test plan execution for staging candidates"
    owner: qa_team
    targets:
      - azure-test-plans

  - id: ado-board-update
    name: "Azure DevOps release board update"
    cron: "0 9 * * 1"
    frequency_note: "Runs on odd-numbered weeks only"
    description: "Sync release items, work items, and test evidence to ADO boards"
    owner: platform_team
    targets:
      - ado-boards

  - id: packaging-readiness-review
    name: "Packaging readiness review"
    cron: "0 15 * * 5"
    frequency_note: "Runs on odd-numbered weeks only"
    description: "Review which assets are ready for next packaging tier"
    owner: platform_team
    requires_approval: true

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Monthly jobs (first Monday of month)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
monthly:
  - id: productized-package-review
    name: "Productized package review"
    cron: "0 10 1-7 * 1"
    description: "Review one candidate for productized packaging upgrade"
    owner: platform_team
    requires_approval: true
    decisions:
      - managed_app_ready
      - saas_ready
      - connector_ready
      - marketplace_ready

  - id: release-retrospective
    name: "Release retrospective"
    cron: "0 14 1-7 * 1"
    description: "Review release velocity, blockers, and process improvements"
    owner: platform_team

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Alert channels
# ----------------------------------------------------------
alerting:
  default_channel: ops-platform-alerts
  escalation_channel: platform-team-urgent
  notification_targets:
    - ops-console-dashboard
    - email-platform-team

schema_version: "1.0"
namespace: data_intelligence.mirroring
last_reviewed: "2026-03-30"
description: >
  SSOT configuration for Microsoft Fabric Mirroring from Odoo PostgreSQL.
  Defines source, target, schema scope, and monitoring thresholds.
  Rehearsal-first: odoo_staging only. Production (odoo) after 48h soak.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Source: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
source:
  server: pg-ipai-odoo
  fqdn: pg-ipai-odoo.postgres.database.azure.com
  resource_group: rg-ipai-dev-odoo-data
  region: southeastasia
  version: "16"
  sku_tier: GeneralPurpose
  key_vault: kv-ipai-dev
  credential_secrets:
    username: odoo-db-user
    password: odoo-db-password

  databases:
    rehearsal:
      name: odoo_staging
      collation: en_US.utf8
      status: rehearsal_target
      notes: >
        First mirroring target. Staging data only. Do not use for production
        BI or Copilot grounding. Rehearsal validates mirroring mechanics.

    production:
      name: odoo
      collation: en_US.utf8
      status: pending_rehearsal_completion
      notes: >
        Production mirroring enabled only after 48h soak of odoo_staging
        succeeds. This is the eventual source for operational dashboards.

    excluded:
      - name: postgres
        reason: system_database
      - name: azure_maintenance
        reason: system_database
      - name: azure_sys
        reason: system_database
      - name: odoo_prod
        reason: deprecated_naming_collision

  required_parameters:
    wal_level: logical
    max_replication_slots: ">= 4"
    max_worker_processes: ">= 8"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schema whitelist: tables to mirror from public schema
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
schema_whitelist:
  mode: all_public
  schema: public
  notes: >
    Mirror all tables in the public schema. Odoo stores all business data
    in the public schema. System tables (pg_*, information_schema) are
    automatically excluded by Fabric.

  # Core business tables (non-exhaustive, for validation reference)
  priority_tables:
    - res_partner
    - res_company
    - res_users
    - account_move
    - account_move_line
    - account_journal
    - account_account
    - sale_order
    - sale_order_line
    - purchase_order
    - purchase_order_line
    - product_template
    - product_product
    - stock_picking
    - stock_move
    - hr_employee
    - hr_expense
    - project_project
    - project_task
    - mail_message

  # Tables excluded from mirroring (if selective mode is used)
  exclusions:
    - pattern: "ir_attachment"
      reason: "Large binary data; not useful for analytics"
    - pattern: "bus_bus"
      reason: "Transient bus messages; no analytical value"
    - pattern: "bus_presence"
      reason: "Transient presence data"
    - pattern: "ir_logging"
      reason: "Server logs; not analytical data"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Target: Microsoft Fabric
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
target:
  platform: microsoft_fabric
  workspace_name: ipai-data-mirror-dev
  workspace_role: tactical_operational_mirror
  capacity:
    name: fcipaidev
    sku: F2
    resource_group: rg-ipai-ai-dev
    region: southeastasia
    status: active
    admin: admin@insightpulseai.com
    provisioned: "2026-03-30"
    notes: >
      F2 capacity provisioned and active. Production workloads
      may require F4 or higher.

  mirrored_database:
    name: odoo-staging-mirror
    format: delta
    landing_layer: bronze
    notes: >
      Mirrored tables land as Delta tables in OneLake. They are treated
      as Bronze-tier data

metadata.title: Source: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

text:
raw operational replicas, not governed
      curated analytics. Reports must NOT consume Bronze directly.

  sql_analytics_endpoint:
    auto_created: true
    notes: >
      Fabric automatically creates a SQL analytics endpoint for each
      mirrored database. Use this for validation queries only during
      rehearsal. Production BI goes through Databricks gold layer.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Governance: how mirrored data relates to the medallion architecture
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
governance:
  data_tier: bronze
  governed: false
  notes: >
    Mirrored data is raw operational replica (Bronze). It has no
    transformation, deduplication, or business key resolution applied.
    It is NOT a substitute for the Databricks medallion pipeline.
    All BI and Copilot consumption must go through Gold tables.

  allowed_consumers:
    - role: data_engineer
      access: read
      purpose: "Exploration, validation, pipeline development"
    - role: platform_admin
      access: read
      purpose: "Monitoring, troubleshooting"

  prohibited_consumers:
    - role: business_analyst
      reason: "Must use Gold layer via Databricks SQL or Power BI semantic model"
    - role: copilot
      reason: "Copilot grounding uses Gold tables only"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monitoring thresholds
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
monitoring:
  replication_lag:
    warning_minutes: 5
    critical_minutes: 15
    notes: "Normal lag is 1-2 minutes. > 5 min indicates issues."

  pg_cpu_impact:
    warning_percent: 5
    critical_percent: 10
    notes: >
      Incremental CPU impact from mirroring. Measured as delta between
      pre-mirroring and post-mirroring baseline. > 10% requires
      investigation (possible WAL contention or slot lag).

  pg_wal_retention:
    warning_gb: 2
    critical_gb: 5
    notes: >
      WAL retained by the replication slot. If mirror is broken or paused,
      WAL accumulates. > 5 GB indicates the slot must be dropped or
      mirroring must be restarted.

  pg_connection_headroom:
    warning_percent: 80
    critical_percent: 90
    notes: "Mirroring uses 1-2 connections. Monitor total pool usage."

  row_count_drift:
    check_interval_hours: 24
    max_drift_percent: 1
    notes: >
      Compare source PG row counts with mirrored table row counts.
      Drift > 1% after initial snapshot indicates replication issues.

  table_replication_status:
    check_interval_hours: 4
    expected: replicating
    notes: "All tables must show 'Replicating' status in Fabric portal."

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constraints
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
constraints:
  ddl_freeze:
    enforced: true
    scope: odoo_staging
    notes: >
      No ALTER TABLE, DROP TABLE, or CREATE TABLE operations on mirrored
      tables while mirroring is active. Coordinate with Odoo module
      installs/upgrades by pausing the mirror first.

  truncate_prohibited:
    enforced: true
    scope: all_mirrored_tables
    alternative: "Use DELETE FROM instead of TRUNCATE"
    notes: "TRUNCATE breaks the mirroring for the affected table."

  server_restart:
    impact: temporary_disconnect
    recovery: automatic
    notes: >
      PG server restart temporarily disconnects mirroring. Mirroring
      auto-reconnects after restart. Verify status in Fabric portal.

  table_ownership:
    required_owner: azure_pg_admin
    notes: >
      All mirrored tables must be owned by azure_pg_admin (or the
      server admin login). Tables owned by other roles are silently
      skipped by Fabric Mirroring.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rehearsal timeline
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
rehearsal:
  phase: go
  target_database: odoo_staging
  soak_period_hours: 48
  success_criteria:
    - "All tables in Replicating status for 48h continuous"
    - "No mirroring errors or restarts during the period"
    - "Replication lag consistently < 5 minutes"
    - "PG CPU impact < 5% incremental"
    - "PG WAL retention not growing unbounded"
    - "Row count delta < 1% at 24h and 48h marks"
    - "CDC change propagation validated at least twice"
  promotion_gate: >
    All success criteria must be met before mirroring is enabled on
    the production 'odoo' database.
  runbook: docs/runbooks/fabric-mirroring-rehearsal.md

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# References
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
references:
  microsoft_docs:
    - title: "Fabric Mirroring for Azure Database for PostgreSQL"
      url: "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/database/mirrored-database/azure-database-postgresql"
    - title: "Fabric Mirroring limitations"
      url: "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/database/mirrored-database/azure-database-postgresql-limitations"
  internal_docs:
    - docs/architecture/DATABRICKS_FABRIC_ROLE_SPLIT.md
    - docs/architecture/data-intelligence-architecture.md
    - docs/architecture/FABRIC_FINANCE_PPM_TARGET_STATE.md
    - docs/runbooks/fabric-capacity-decision.md
    - ssot/odoo/runtime_contract.yaml

metadata.title: Source: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

text:
# =============================================================================
# Data Intelligence Platform Contract — SSOT
# =============================================================================
# Machine-readable authority surface for the data intelligence plane.
# Prose equivalent: README.md (platform contract table) and CLAUDE.md (rules).
#
# Ref: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azurearchitectureblog/data-intelligence-end-to-end-with-azure-databricks-and-microsoft-fabric/4232621
# =============================================================================

schema_version: 1
kind: data_intelligence_platform_contract
last_reviewed: "2026-03-26"

lakehouse_platform: databricks
cloud: azure

storage_platform:
  primary: adls_gen2
  format: delta_lake

governance:
  primary_catalog: unity_catalog
  metadata_visibility_plane: purview

consumption:
  primary_bi_plane: power_bi_fabric
  semantic_serving_plane: databricks_sql
  fabric_role: downstream_consumption_only

ingestion:
  streaming:
    primary: azure_event_hubs
  batch:
    primary: auto_loader
  relational:
    primary: lakehouse_federation
  operational_mirroring:
    primary: fabric_database_mirroring
    source: azure_postgresql_odoo
  app_cdc:
    primary: supabase_etl_rust
    destination: apache_iceberg

modeling_pattern:
  - bronze
  - silver
  - gold
  - platinum

source_systems:
  transactional_sor:
    - odoo
  control_plane:
    - supabase
  orchestration:
    - n8n

platform_controls:
  identity: entra_id
  secrets: azure_key_vault
  monitoring: azure_monitor
  cicd:
    - github
    - azure_devops

architecture_doctrine:
  databricks_is_canonical_engineering_plane: true
  fabric_is_canonical_engineering_plane: false
  fabric_is_consumption_plane: true
  unity_catalog_governs_all_production_assets: true
  n8n_is_orchestration_only: true

# ============================================================
# RELEASE LANES — Cadence & Promotion Model
# ============================================================
# Three lanes only. Separates internal iteration from
# controlled promotion from productized packaging.
# Last updated: 2026-03-15
# ============================================================

schema_version: "1.0"

lanes:

  - id: lane_a
    name: "Internal Capability Releases"
    cadence: weekly
    description: >
      Fast iteration on internal capabilities. No external
      visibility. Safe to break and fix quickly.
    examples:
      - "Odoo Copilot preview improvements"
      - "DocFlow alpha iteration"
      - "Ops Console new screens"
      - "Connector prototypes"
    allowed_channels:
      - internal_one_click
      - internal_repo_artifacts
    gates:
      - smoke_evals_pass
      - no_policy_violations
    approval_required: false

  - id: lane_b
    name: "Controlled Promotion Releases"
    cadence: biweekly
    description: >
      Gated promotions from dev to staging. Requires eval
      pass and UAT signoff. No external publishing.
    examples:
      - "Dev → staging promotions"
      - "Gated write-mode expansions"
      - "UAT-approved feature packs"
    allowed_channels:
      - internal_one_click
      - internal_service
    gates:
      - full_eval_pack_pass
      - uat_signoff
      - policy_compliance
      - no_critical_blockers
    approval_required: true

  - id: lane_c
    name: "Productized Packaging Releases"
    cadence: monthly_or_slower
    description: >
      Formal packaging for managed apps, connectors, SaaS,
      or marketplace. Requires stable internal usage first.
    examples:
      - "Managed app package"
      - "External connector package"
      - "Commercial offer package"
    allowed_channels:
      - azure_managed_application
      - connector_plugin_extension
      - hosted_saas
      - azure_marketplace
    gates:
      - stable_internal_usage_30d
      - full_eval_pack_pass
      - uat_signoff
      - security_review
      - packaging_review
      - arm_template_valid
    approval_required: true

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Promotion rules
# ----------------------------------------------------------
promotion_rules:
  - from: lane_a
    to: lane_b
    requires:
      - "7+ days in lane_a without regression"
      - "Smoke evals green for 5 consecutive days"
      - "No open P0/P1 issues"

  - from: lane_b
    to: lane_c
    requires:
      - "14+ days in lane_b without regression"
      - "UAT signoff complete"
      - "Security review passed"
      - "Packaging readiness review approved"

# ============================================================
# RELEASE REGISTRY — Priority Release Ladder
# ============================================================
# Source of truth for all release milestones, packaging,
# and promotion gates.
# Last updated: 2026-03-15
# ============================================================

schema_version: "1.0"

text:
# Compute Comparison Matrix — Serverless vs Pro Clusters
# Used by medallion capacity test to benchmark execution modes

compute_modes:
  serverless:
    description: "Databricks serverless job compute"
    config:
      # Serverless uses Databricks-managed infrastructure
      # No cluster config needed — platform auto-provisions
      compute_type: serverless
    expected_characteristics:
      startup_time: "< 30s"
      autoscale: "automatic, platform-managed"
      cost_model: "DBU per task-second"
      concurrency: "high (platform-managed)"
      best_for:
        - small-to-medium batch jobs
        - variable/bursty workloads
        - jobs that need fast startup
        - cost-sensitive dev/test runs

  pro:
    description: "Databricks pro/classic job cluster"
    config:
      spark_version: "15.4.x-scala2.12"
      node_type_id: "Standard_DS3_v2"
      autoscale:
        min_workers: 2
        max_workers: 8
      first_on_demand: 1
      availability: ON_DEMAND_AZURE
    expected_characteristics:
      startup_time: "2-5 minutes"
      autoscale: "min/max workers, manual policy"
      cost_model: "DBU per VM-hour"
      concurrency: "bounded by cluster size"
      best_for:
        - large sustained workloads
        - workloads needing specific node types
        - GPU/ML training
        - streaming with fixed clusters

# Metrics to compare across modes
comparison_metrics:
  - name: startup_time_seconds
    description: "Time from job submission to first task execution"
    unit: seconds

  - name: ingest_throughput_rows_sec
    description: "Bronze ingestion rows per second"
    unit: rows/sec

  - name: transform_duration_seconds
    description: "Silver transform total wall-clock time"
    unit: seconds

  - name: gold_build_duration_seconds
    description: "Gold aggregate build time"
    unit: seconds

  - name: query_latency_p50_ms
    description: "50th percentile Gold query latency"
    unit: milliseconds

  - name: query_latency_p99_ms
    description: "99th percentile Gold query latency"
    unit: milliseconds

  - name: total_dbu_consumed
    description: "Total DBUs consumed for full pipeline run"
    unit: DBU

  - name: total_wall_clock_seconds
    description: "End-to-end pipeline wall-clock time"
    unit: seconds

  - name: shuffle_spill_bytes
    description: "Total shuffle spill to disk"
    unit: bytes

  - name: failure_count
    description: "Number of task failures/retries"
    unit: count

# Decision matrix output
decision_template:
  workload_class: ""  # ingest-heavy, transform-heavy, query-heavy, mixed
  recommended_mode: ""  # serverless or pro
  rationale: ""
  breakpoint_rows: 0  # row count where mode switch is recommended
  cost_ratio: 0.0  # serverless_cost / pro_cost

text:
"""
Synthetic data generator for medallion pipeline stress testing.

Generates five tables — customers, invoices, line_items, payments, events —
with configurable scale profiles. Produces DataFrames (PySpark or pandas)
or writes CSV/JSON for offline use.

Designed for Databricks Runtime 15.4+ / PySpark 3.5+.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import math
import os
import random
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import yaml

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Profile loader
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_HERE = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
_DEFAULT_PROFILES = _HERE / "profiles.yaml"


@dataclass
class ScaleProfile:
    """Parsed scale profile from profiles.yaml."""

    name: str
    rows_per_table: int
    line_items_per_invoice: int
    payments_ratio: float
    events_per_customer: int
    duplicate_rate: float
    null_rate: float
    late_arriving_rate: float
    skew_intensity: float
    schema_drift: bool


def load_profile(name: str = "small", path: Optional[str] = None) -> ScaleProfile:
    """Load a named profile from the YAML config."""
    p = Path(path) if path else _DEFAULT_PROFILES
    with open(p) as f:
        cfg = yaml.safe_load(f)
    raw = cfg["profiles"][name]
    return ScaleProfile(name=name, **raw)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deterministic helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_FIRST_NAMES = [
    "Maria", "Juan", "Jose", "Ana", "Luis", "Rosa", "Carlos", "Elena",
    "Miguel", "Teresa", "Pedro", "Carmen", "Rafael", "Sofia", "Antonio",
    "Isabella", "Francisco", "Lucia", "Andres", "Patricia",
]
_LAST_NAMES = [
    "Santos", "Reyes", "Cruz", "Garcia", "Lopez", "Martinez", "Rivera",
    "Hernandez", "Torres", "Flores", "Ramos", "Dela Cruz", "Gonzales",
    "Bautista", "Villanueva", "Mendoza", "Castillo", "Aquino", "Soriano", "Lim",
]
_CURRENCIES = ["PHP", "USD", "EUR"]
_PAYMENT_METHODS = ["bank_transfer", "credit_card", "gcash", "maya", "cash"]
_EVENT_TYPES = [
    "page_view", "login", "invoice_created", "payment_received",
    "email_sent", "report_generated", "module_installed", "error",
]
_PRODUCT_NAMES = [
    "Consulting Hour", "Cloud Hosting (mo)", "Data Pipeline Setup",
    "Custom Module Dev", "Support Ticket", "Training Session",
    "License (annual)", "Integration Fee", "Audit Service", "Report Pack",
]
_REGIONS = ["NCR", "Region III", "Region IV-A", "Region VII", "Region XI"]

"Lim", ] _CURRENCIES = ["PHP", "USD", "EUR"] _PAYMENT_METHODS = ["bank_transfer", "credit_card", "gcash", "maya", "cash"] _EVENT_TYPES = [ "page_view", "login", "invoice_created", "payment_received", "email_sent", "report_generated", "module_installed", "error", ] _PRODUCT_NAMES = [ "Consulting Hour", "Cloud Hosting (mo)", "Data Pipeline Setup", "Custom Module Dev", "Support Ticket", "Training Session", "License (annual)", "Integration Fee", "Audit Service", "Report Pack", ] _REGIONS = ["NCR", "Region III", "Region IV-A", "Region VII", "Region XI"]


def _pareto_index(n: int, skew: float, rng: random.Random) -> int:
    """Return an index [0, n) with Pareto-like skew (0 = uniform, 1 = heavy)."""
    if skew <= 0 or n <= 1:
        return rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    # Use inverse power-law transform
    alpha = 1.0 + (1.0 - skew) * 4  # alpha from 1 (heavy) to 5 (light)
    u = rng.random()
    raw = int(n * (1 - u ** (1 / alpha)))
    return min(raw, n - 1)


def _maybe_null(value: Any, null_rate: float, rng: random.Random) -> Any:
    """Return None with probability null_rate, else return value."""
    if rng.random() < null_rate:
        return None
    return value


def _random_date(start: datetime, end: datetime, rng: random.Random) -> datetime:
    delta = (end - start).total_seconds()
    return start + timedelta(seconds=rng.random() * delta)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table generators — return list[dict]
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def generate_customers(
    profile: ScaleProfile,
    seed: int = 42,
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Generate synthetic customer records."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    n = profile.rows_per_table
    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    for i in range(n):
        cust_id = str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4))
        first = rng.choice(_FIRST_NAMES)
        last = rng.choice(_LAST_NAMES)
        rec = {
            "customer_id": cust_id,
            "name": f"{first} {last}",
            "email": _maybe_null(
                f"{first.lower()}.{last.lower().replace(' ', '')}_{i}@example.com",
                profile.null_rate,
                rng,
            ),
            "phone": _maybe_null(f"+639{rng.randint(100000000, 999999999)}", profile.null_rate, rng),
            "region": rng.choice(_REGIONS),
            "currency": rng.choice(_CURRENCIES),
            "is_active": rng.random() > 0.1,
            "created_at": _random_date(
                datetime(2023, 1, 1), datetime(2026, 3, 1), rng
            ).isoformat(),
        }

        # Schema drift: extra column not in canonical schema
        if profile.schema_drift and rng.random() < 0.3:
            rec["_drift_loyalty_tier"] = rng.choice(["bronze", "silver", "gold"])

        records.append(rec)

    # Inject duplicates
    dup_count = int(n * profile.duplicate_rate)
    for _ in range(dup_count):
        records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, n - 1)]))

profile.null_rate, rng, ), "phone": _maybe_null(f"+639{rng.randint(100000000, 999999999)}", profile.null_rate, rng), "region": rng.choice(_REGIONS), "currency": rng.choice(_CURRENCIES), "is_active": rng.random() > 0.1, "created_at": _random_date( datetime(2023, 1, 1), datetime(2026, 3, 1), rng ).isoformat(), } # Schema drift: extra column not in canonical schema if profile.schema_drift and rng.random() < 0.3: rec["_drift_loyalty_tier"] = rng.choice(["bronze", "silver", "gold"]) records.append(rec) # Inject duplicates dup_count = int(n * profile.duplicate_rate) for _ in range(dup_count): records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, n - 1)]))

    rng.shuffle(records)
    return records


def generate_invoices(
    profile: ScaleProfile,
    customer_ids: List[str],
    seed: int = 43,
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Generate synthetic invoice headers."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    n = profile.rows_per_table
    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    for i in range(n):
        cust_idx = _pareto_index(len(customer_ids), profile.skew_intensity, rng)
        invoice_date = _random_date(datetime(2024, 1, 1), datetime(2026, 3, 15), rng)
        ingest_ts = invoice_date + timedelta(hours=rng.randint(0, 4))

        # Late-arriving fact
        if rng.random() < profile.late_arriving_rate:
            ingest_ts = invoice_date + timedelta(days=rng.randint(2, 30))

        rec = {
            "invoice_id": str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4)),
            "customer_id": customer_ids[cust_idx],
            "invoice_number": f"INV-{2024 + i // 5000}-{i:06d}",
            "invoice_date": invoice_date.date().isoformat(),
            "due_date": (invoice_date + timedelta(days=rng.choice([15, 30, 45, 60]))).date().isoformat(),
            "currency": rng.choice(_CURRENCIES),
            "status": rng.choice(["draft", "posted", "paid", "cancelled"]),
            "total_amount": None,  # will be computed from line items
            "ingest_ts": ingest_ts.isoformat(),
        }

        if profile.schema_drift and rng.random() < 0.2:
            rec["_drift_po_number"] = f"PO-{rng.randint(1000, 9999)}"

        records.append(rec)

    # Duplicates
    dup_count = int(n * profile.duplicate_rate)
    for _ in range(dup_count):
        records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, n - 1)]))

    rng.shuffle(records)
    return records


def generate_line_items(
    profile: ScaleProfile,
    invoice_ids: List[str],
    seed: int = 44,
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Generate invoice line items. Count per invoice follows profile avg."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    avg = profile.line_items_per_invoice

if profile.schema_drift and rng.random() < 0.2: rec["_drift_po_number"] = f"PO-{rng.randint(1000, 9999)}" records.append(rec) # Duplicates dup_count = int(n * profile.duplicate_rate) for _ in range(dup_count): records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, n - 1)])) rng.shuffle(records) return records def generate_line_items( profile: ScaleProfile, invoice_ids: List[str], seed: int = 44, ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: """Generate invoice line items. Count per invoice follows profile avg.""" rng = random.Random(seed) records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [] avg = profile.line_items_per_invoice

    for inv_id in invoice_ids:
        count = max(1, int(rng.gauss(avg, avg * 0.4)))
        for seq in range(count):
            qty = rng.randint(1, 20)
            unit_price = round(rng.uniform(50, 50000), 2)
            discount = _maybe_null(round(rng.uniform(0, 0.25), 4), profile.null_rate, rng)
            rec = {
                "line_item_id": str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4)),
                "invoice_id": inv_id,
                "sequence": seq + 1,
                "product_name": rng.choice(_PRODUCT_NAMES),
                "quantity": qty,
                "unit_price": unit_price,
                "discount_pct": discount,
                "line_total": round(qty * unit_price * (1 - (discount or 0)), 2),
            }
            records.append(rec)

    # Duplicate a fraction of lines
    dup_count = int(len(records) * profile.duplicate_rate)
    for _ in range(dup_count):
        records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, len(records) - 1)]))

    rng.shuffle(records)
    return records


def generate_payments(
    profile: ScaleProfile,
    invoice_ids: List[str],
    seed: int = 45,
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Generate payment records for a fraction of invoices."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    n_pay = int(len(invoice_ids) * profile.payments_ratio)
    selected = rng.sample(invoice_ids, min(n_pay, len(invoice_ids)))
    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    for inv_id in selected:
        pay_date = _random_date(datetime(2024, 2, 1), datetime(2026, 3, 20), rng)
        rec = {
            "payment_id": str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4)),
            "invoice_id": inv_id,
            "payment_date": pay_date.date().isoformat(),
            "amount": round(rng.uniform(500, 100000), 2),
            "method": rng.choice(_PAYMENT_METHODS),
            "reference": _maybe_null(f"REF-{rng.randint(100000, 999999)}", profile.null_rate, rng),
        }
        records.append(rec)

    dup_count = int(len(records) * profile.duplicate_rate)
    for _ in range(dup_count):
        records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, len(records) - 1)]))

    rng.shuffle(records)
    return records


def generate_events(
    profile: ScaleProfile,
    customer_ids: List[str],
    seed: int = 46,
) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Generate customer event/activity log."""
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

= { "payment_id": str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4)), "invoice_id": inv_id, "payment_date": pay_date.date().isoformat(), "amount": round(rng.uniform(500, 100000), 2), "method": rng.choice(_PAYMENT_METHODS), "reference": _maybe_null(f"REF-{rng.randint(100000, 999999)}", profile.null_rate, rng), } records.append(rec) dup_count = int(len(records) * profile.duplicate_rate) for _ in range(dup_count): records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, len(records) - 1)])) rng.shuffle(records) return records def generate_events( profile: ScaleProfile, customer_ids: List[str], seed: int = 46, ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: """Generate customer event/activity log.""" rng = random.Random(seed) records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    for cust_id in customer_ids:
        count = max(1, int(rng.gauss(profile.events_per_customer, 2)))
        for _ in range(count):
            event_ts = _random_date(datetime(2024, 6, 1), datetime(2026, 3, 20), rng)
            ingest_ts = event_ts + timedelta(seconds=rng.randint(0, 300))
            if rng.random() < profile.late_arriving_rate:
                ingest_ts = event_ts + timedelta(days=rng.randint(1, 14))

            rec = {
                "event_id": str(uuid.UUID(int=rng.getrandbits(128), version=4)),
                "customer_id": cust_id,
                "event_type": rng.choice(_EVENT_TYPES),
                "event_ts": event_ts.isoformat(),
                "ingest_ts": ingest_ts.isoformat(),
                "payload": _maybe_null(
                    json.dumps({"page": f"/app/{rng.randint(1,50)}", "duration_ms": rng.randint(100, 30000)}),
                    profile.null_rate,
                    rng,
                ),
            }
            records.append(rec)

    dup_count = int(len(records) * profile.duplicate_rate)
    for _ in range(dup_count):
        records.append(dict(records[rng.randint(0, len(records) - 1)]))

    rng.shuffle(records)
    return records


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Orchestrator — generates all tables in one call
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class SyntheticDataset:
    """Container for all generated tables."""

    profile: ScaleProfile
    customers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    invoices: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    line_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    payments: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    events: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    generation_ts: str = ""

    def summary(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
        return {
            "customers": len(self.customers),
            "invoices": len(self.invoices),
            "line_items": len(self.line_items),
            "payments": len(self.payments),
            "events": len(self.events),
        }

all tables in one call # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- @dataclass class SyntheticDataset: """Container for all generated tables.""" profile: ScaleProfile customers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list) invoices: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list) line_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list) payments: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list) events: List[Dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list) generation_ts: str = "" def summary(self) -> Dict[str, int]: return { "customers": len(self.customers), "invoices": len(self.invoices), "line_items": len(self.line_items), "payments": len(self.payments), "events": len(self.events), }


def generate_all(
    profile_name: str = "small",
    profile_path: Optional[str] = None,
    seed: int = 42,
) -> SyntheticDataset:
    """Generate a complete synthetic dataset for all five tables."""
    profile = load_profile(profile_name, profile_path)
    ds = SyntheticDataset(profile=profile, generation_ts=datetime.utcnow().isoformat())

    ds.customers = generate_customers(profile, seed=seed)
    customer_ids = [c["customer_id"] for c in ds.customers if c.get("customer_id")]
    # Deduplicate customer_ids for FK references
    customer_ids = list(dict.fromkeys(customer_ids))

    ds.invoices = generate_invoices(profile, customer_ids, seed=seed + 1)
    invoice_ids = [inv["invoice_id"] for inv in ds.invoices if inv.get("invoice_id")]
    invoice_ids = list(dict.fromkeys(invoice_ids))

    ds.line_items = generate_line_items(profile, invoice_ids, seed=seed + 2)
    ds.payments = generate_payments(profile, invoice_ids, seed=seed + 3)
    ds.events = generate_events(profile, customer_ids, seed=seed + 4)

    # Backfill invoice total_amount from line items
    totals: Dict[str, float] = {}
    for li in ds.line_items:
        inv = li.get("invoice_id")
        if inv:
            totals[inv] = totals.get(inv, 0) + (li.get("line_total") or 0)
    for inv in ds.invoices:
        inv["total_amount"] = totals.get(inv["invoice_id"], 0)

    return ds


def write_csv(dataset: SyntheticDataset, output_dir: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """Write all tables to CSV files. Returns {table: path}."""
    import csv

    out = Path(output_dir)
    out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    paths: Dict[str, str] = {}

    for table_name in ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]:
        rows = getattr(dataset, table_name)
        if not rows:
            continue
        # Union all keys across rows (handles schema drift)
        all_keys = list(dict.fromkeys(k for row in rows for k in row))
        fpath = out / f"{table_name}.csv"
        with open(fpath, "w", newline="") as f:
            writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=all_keys, extrasaction="ignore")
            writer.writeheader()
            writer.writerows(rows)
        paths[table_name] = str(fpath)

    return paths

paths: Dict[str, str] = {} for table_name in ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]: rows = getattr(dataset, table_name) if not rows: continue # Union all keys across rows (handles schema drift) all_keys = list(dict.fromkeys(k for row in rows for k in row)) fpath = out / f"{table_name}.csv" with open(fpath, "w", newline="") as f: writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=all_keys, extrasaction="ignore") writer.writeheader() writer.writerows(rows) paths[table_name] = str(fpath) return paths


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI entry point (for local testing outside Databricks)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate synthetic medallion test data")
    parser.add_argument("--profile", default="small", choices=["tiny", "small", "medium", "large"])
    parser.add_argument("--output", default="/tmp/synthetic_data")
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
    parser.add_argument("--format", default="csv", choices=["csv", "json"])
    args = parser.parse_args()

    ds = generate_all(profile_name=args.profile, seed=args.seed)
    print(f"Generated dataset ({args.profile}): {ds.summary()}")

    if args.format == "csv":
        paths = write_csv(ds, args.output)
        for table, path in paths.items():
            print(f"  {table}: {path}")
    else:
        out = Path(args.output)
        out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        for table_name in ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]:
            rows = getattr(ds, table_name)
            fpath = out / f"{table_name}.json"
            with open(fpath, "w") as f:
                json.dump(rows, f, indent=2, default=str)
            print(f"  {table_name}: {fpath}")

# Synthetic data scale profiles for medallion pipeline stress testing.
# Used by data_generator.py and seed_synthetic notebook.
#
# Fields:
#   rows_per_table  — base row count for primary tables (customers, invoices)
#   line_items_per_invoice — average line items per invoice
#   payments_ratio  — fraction of invoices that have a payment
#   events_per_customer — average events per customer
#   duplicate_rate   — fraction of rows that are exact duplicates (Bronze realism)
#   null_rate        — fraction of nullable fields set to NULL
#   late_arriving_rate — fraction of records with ingest_ts > event_ts by >24h
#   skew_intensity   — 0.0 (uniform) to 1.0 (80/20 Pareto on key columns)
#   schema_drift     — inject extra columns not in canonical schema (bool)

profiles:
  tiny:
    rows_per_table: 100
    line_items_per_invoice: 2
    payments_ratio: 0.6
    events_per_customer: 3
    duplicate_rate: 0.05
    null_rate: 0.02
    late_arriving_rate: 0.01
    skew_intensity: 0.3
    schema_drift: false

  small:
    rows_per_table: 1000
    line_items_per_invoice: 3
    payments_ratio: 0.7
    events_per_customer: 5
    duplicate_rate: 0.08
    null_rate: 0.05
    late_arriving_rate: 0.03
    skew_intensity: 0.5
    schema_drift: true

  medium:
    rows_per_table: 10000
    line_items_per_invoice: 4
    payments_ratio: 0.75
    events_per_customer: 8
    duplicate_rate: 0.10
    null_rate: 0.07
    late_arriving_rate: 0.05
    skew_intensity: 0.7
    schema_drift: true

  large:
    rows_per_table: 100000
    line_items_per_invoice: 5
    payments_ratio: 0.80
    events_per_customer: 12
    duplicate_rate: 0.12
    null_rate: 0.10
    late_arriving_rate: 0.08
    skew_intensity: 0.9
    schema_drift: true

text:
# Unity Catalog Namespace Contract — Medallion Capacity Test
# All test assets are isolated from production schemas

namespace:
  catalog: dbw_ipai_dev
  test_schemas:
    - test_bronze    # Raw synthetic data landing
    - test_silver    # Cleansed/conformed transforms
    - test_gold      # Business aggregates/marts
    - test_qa        # Quality metrics and validation results

  # NEVER use these production schemas for testing
  prohibited_schemas:
    - default
    - bronze         # Production Bronze
    - silver         # Production Silver
    - gold           # Production Gold
    - information_schema

naming_convention:
  tables: "{schema}.{entity}_{profile}"  # e.g., test_bronze.customers_small
  volumes: "test_volumes_{profile}"
  checkpoints: "test_checkpoints_{run_id}"

cleanup_policy:
  method: "DROP SCHEMA ... CASCADE"
  timing: "after each test run (unless cleanup=false)"
  guardrails:
    - "Verify schema name starts with 'test_' before DROP"
    - "Never DROP schemas not in test_schemas list"
    - "Log all DROP operations to test_qa.cleanup_log"

grants:
  # Test schemas inherit catalog-level grants
  # No additional grants needed for workspace users with USE CATALOG
  required_catalog_privilege: "USE CATALOG"
  required_schema_privilege: "CREATE TABLE, USE SCHEMA"

tags:
  environment: test
  managed_by: medallion-capacity-harness
  auto_cleanup: true

text:
"""
Medallion pipeline validation suite.

Can be run as a standalone script (with PySpark) or imported into a
Databricks notebook. Validates row counts, schema contracts, aggregate
correctness, and idempotent reruns.

Usage (Databricks):
    from tests.synthetic.validate_medallion import MedallionValidator
    v = MedallionValidator(spark, catalog="dbw_ipai_dev")
    results = v.run_all()
    v.print_report(results)

Usage (CLI — requires local Spark):
    python -m tests.synthetic.validate_medallion
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

# PySpark imports — will fail gracefully outside Spark env
try:
    from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, DataFrame
    from pyspark.sql import functions as F

    HAS_SPARK = True
except ImportError:
    HAS_SPARK = False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schema contracts
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SILVER_SCHEMAS = {
    "customers": {
        "required": ["customer_id", "name", "region", "currency", "is_active", "created_at"],
        "optional": ["email", "phone"],
        "types": {
            "customer_id": "string",
            "name": "string",
            "is_active": "boolean",
            "created_at": "timestamp",
        },
    },
    "invoices": {
        "required": ["invoice_id", "customer_id", "invoice_date", "due_date", "status", "total_amount", "ingest_ts"],
        "optional": ["invoice_number", "currency", "is_late_arriving"],
        "types": {
            "invoice_id": "string",
            "customer_id": "string",
            "invoice_date": "date",
            "due_date": "date",
            "total_amount": "double",
            "ingest_ts": "timestamp",
        },
    },
    "line_items": {
        "required": ["line_item_id", "invoice_id", "sequence", "product_name", "quantity", "unit_price", "line_total"],
        "optional": ["discount_pct"],
        "types": {
            "line_item_id": "string",
            "invoice_id": "string",
            "quantity": "int",
            "unit_price": "double",
            "line_total": "double",
        },
    },
    "payments": {
        "required": ["payment_id", "invoice_id", "payment_date", "amount", "method"],
        "optional": ["reference"],
        "types": {
            "payment_id": "string",
            "invoice_id": "string",
            "payment_date": "date",
            "amount": "double",
        },
    },
    "events": {
        "required": ["event_id", "customer_id", "event_type", "event_ts", "ingest_ts"],
        "optional": ["payload", "is_late_arriving"],
        "types": {
            "event_id": "string",
            "customer_id": "string",
            "event_ts": "timestamp",
            "ingest_ts": "timestamp",
        },
    },
}

GOLD_TABLES = ["revenue_summary", "customer_kpis", "payment_aging"]
