{ "line_item_id": "string", "invoice_id": "string", "quantity": "int", "unit_price": "double", "line_total": "double", }, }, "payments": { "required": ["payment_id", "invoice_id", "payment_date", "amount", "method"], "optional": ["reference"], "types": { "payment_id": "string", "invoice_id": "string", "payment_date": "date", "amount": "double", }, }, "events": { "required": ["event_id", "customer_id", "event_type", "event_ts", "ingest_ts"], "optional": ["payload", "is_late_arriving"], "types": { "event_id": "string", "customer_id": "string", "event_ts": "timestamp", "ingest_ts": "timestamp", }, }, } GOLD_TABLES = ["revenue_summary", "customer_kpis", "payment_aging"]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validation result types
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class CheckResult:
    """Single validation check result."""

    check_name: str
    passed: bool
    detail: str
    severity: str = "error"  # error | warning


@dataclass
class ValidationReport:
    """Full validation report."""

    run_ts: str = ""
    catalog: str = ""
    checks: List[CheckResult] = field(default_factory=list)
    passed: int = 0
    failed: int = 0
    warnings: int = 0

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "run_ts": self.run_ts,
            "catalog": self.catalog,
            "total_checks": len(self.checks),
            "passed": self.passed,
            "failed": self.failed,
            "warnings": self.warnings,
            "all_passed": self.failed == 0,
            "checks": [
                {"name": c.check_name, "passed": c.passed, "detail": c.detail, "severity": c.severity}
                for c in self.checks
            ],
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Validator
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class MedallionValidator:
    """Validates medallion pipeline outputs across Bronze, Silver, Gold."""

    def __init__(self, spark: "SparkSession", catalog: str = "dbw_ipai_dev"):
        self.spark = spark
        self.catalog = catalog
        self.bronze = f"{catalog}.test_bronze"
        self.silver = f"{catalog}.test_silver"
        self.gold = f"{catalog}.test_gold"

    def _table_exists(self, fqn: str) -> bool:
        try:
            self.spark.table(fqn)
            return True
        except Exception:
            return False

    def _count(self, fqn: str) -> int:
        return self.spark.table(fqn).count()

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Check: Row count invariants
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

class MedallionValidator: """Validates medallion pipeline outputs across Bronze, Silver, Gold.""" def __init__(self, spark: "SparkSession", catalog: str = "dbw_ipai_dev"): self.spark = spark self.catalog = catalog self.bronze = f"{catalog}.test_bronze" self.silver = f"{catalog}.test_silver" self.gold = f"{catalog}.test_gold" def _table_exists(self, fqn: str) -> bool: try: self.spark.table(fqn) return True except Exception: return False def _count(self, fqn: str) -> int: return self.spark.table(fqn).count() # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- # Check: Row count invariants # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def check_row_counts(self) -> List[CheckResult]:
        """Bronze >= Silver (after dedup). Gold is aggregated (fewer rows expected)."""
        results = []
        for table in ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]:
            bronze_fqn = f"{self.bronze}.{table}"
            silver_fqn = f"{self.silver}.{table}"

            if not self._table_exists(bronze_fqn):
                results.append(CheckResult(f"row_count.{table}.bronze_exists", False, "Bronze table missing"))
                continue
            if not self._table_exists(silver_fqn):
                results.append(CheckResult(f"row_count.{table}.silver_exists", False, "Silver table missing"))
                continue

            b = self._count(bronze_fqn)
            s = self._count(silver_fqn)
            ok = b >= s
            results.append(CheckResult(
                f"row_count.{table}.bronze_gte_silver",
                ok,
                f"Bronze={b:,} Silver={s:,} (dedup removed {b - s:,})",
            ))

        # Gold tables should exist and have > 0 rows
        for table in GOLD_TABLES:
            gold_fqn = f"{self.gold}.{table}"
            if not self._table_exists(gold_fqn):
                results.append(CheckResult(f"row_count.{table}.gold_exists", False, "Gold table missing"))
                continue
            g = self._count(gold_fqn)
            results.append(CheckResult(
                f"row_count.{table}.gold_not_empty",
                g > 0,
                f"Gold rows={g:,}",
            ))

        return results

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Check: Schema contracts
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def check_schemas(self) -> List[CheckResult]:
        """Validate Silver tables have required columns with correct types."""
        results = []
        for table, contract in SILVER_SCHEMAS.items():
            fqn = f"{self.silver}.{table}"
            if not self._table_exists(fqn):
                results.append(CheckResult(f"schema.{table}.exists", False, "Table missing"))
                continue

            df = self.spark.table(fqn)
            actual_cols = {f.name: f.dataType.simpleString() for f in df.schema.fields}

g = self._count(gold_fqn) results.append(CheckResult( f"row_count.{table}.gold_not_empty", g > 0, f"Gold rows={g:,}", )) return results # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- # Check: Schema contracts # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- def check_schemas(self) -> List[CheckResult]: """Validate Silver tables have required columns with correct types.""" results = [] for table, contract in SILVER_SCHEMAS.items(): fqn = f"{self.silver}.{table}" if not self._table_exists(fqn): results.append(CheckResult(f"schema.{table}.exists", False, "Table missing")) continue df = self.spark.table(fqn) actual_cols = {f.name: f.dataType.simpleString() for f in df.schema.fields}

            # Required columns present
            for col in contract["required"]:
                present = col in actual_cols
                results.append(CheckResult(
                    f"schema.{table}.has_{col}",
                    present,
                    f"{'present' if present else 'MISSING'} in {table}",
                ))

            # Type checks
            for col, expected_type in contract.get("types", {}).items():
                if col not in actual_cols:
                    continue
                actual = actual_cols[col]
                ok = expected_type in actual  # e.g. "double" in "double", "string" in "string"
                results.append(CheckResult(
                    f"schema.{table}.{col}_type",
                    ok,
                    f"expected={expected_type} actual={actual}",
                    severity="error" if not ok else "error",
                ))

            # No drift columns leaked into Silver
            drift_cols = [c for c in actual_cols if c.startswith("_drift_")]
            results.append(CheckResult(
                f"schema.{table}.no_drift_cols",
                len(drift_cols) == 0,
                f"drift columns: {drift_cols}" if drift_cols else "clean",
            ))

        return results

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Check: Silver uniqueness (no duplicates on PK)
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

actual # e.g. "double" in "double", "string" in "string" results.append(CheckResult( f"schema.{table}.{col}_type", ok, f"expected={expected_type} actual={actual}", severity="error" if not ok else "error", )) # No drift columns leaked into Silver drift_cols = [c for c in actual_cols if c.startswith("_drift_")] results.append(CheckResult( f"schema.{table}.no_drift_cols", len(drift_cols) == 0, f"drift columns: {drift_cols}" if drift_cols else "clean", )) return results # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- # Check: Silver uniqueness (no duplicates on PK) # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def check_uniqueness(self) -> List[CheckResult]:
        """Verify Silver tables have no duplicate primary keys."""
        pk_map = {
            "customers": "customer_id",
            "invoices": "invoice_id",
            "line_items": "line_item_id",
            "payments": "payment_id",
            "events": "event_id",
        }
        results = []
        for table, pk in pk_map.items():
            fqn = f"{self.silver}.{table}"
            if not self._table_exists(fqn):
                continue
            df = self.spark.table(fqn)
            total = df.count()
            distinct = df.select(pk).distinct().count()
            ok = total == distinct
            results.append(CheckResult(
                f"uniqueness.{table}.{pk}",
                ok,
                f"total={total:,} distinct={distinct:,} dupes={total - distinct:,}",
            ))
        return results

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Check: Gold aggregate correctness
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def check_gold_correctness(self) -> List[CheckResult]:
        """Cross-check Gold aggregates against Silver source."""
        results = []

        # Revenue summary: total revenue should match sum of line_totals
        # for posted/paid invoices
        if self._table_exists(f"{self.gold}.revenue_summary") and self._table_exists(f"{self.silver}.invoices"):
            gold_total = (
                self.spark.table(f"{self.gold}.revenue_summary")
                .agg(F.sum("total_revenue").alias("total"))
                .collect()[0]["total"]
            ) or 0

            silver_total = (
                self.spark.table(f"{self.silver}.invoices")
                .filter(F.col("status").isin("posted", "paid"))
                .join(self.spark.table(f"{self.silver}.line_items"), "invoice_id", "inner")
                .agg(F.sum("line_total").alias("total"))
                .collect()[0]["total"]
            ) or 0

            # Allow 0.01 tolerance for floating point
            diff = abs(gold_total - silver_total)
            ok = diff < 0.01
            results.append(CheckResult(
                "gold_correctness.revenue_total",
                ok,
                f"gold={gold_total:,.2f} silver_ref={silver_total:,.2f} diff={diff:,.2f}",
            ))

Revenue summary: total revenue should match sum of line_totals # for posted/paid invoices if self._table_exists(f"{self.gold}.revenue_summary") and self._table_exists(f"{self.silver}.invoices"): gold_total = ( self.spark.table(f"{self.gold}.revenue_summary") .agg(F.sum("total_revenue").alias("total")) .collect()[0]["total"] ) or 0 silver_total = ( self.spark.table(f"{self.silver}.invoices") .filter(F.col("status").isin("posted", "paid")) .join(self.spark.table(f"{self.silver}.line_items"), "invoice_id", "inner") .agg(F.sum("line_total").alias("total")) .collect()[0]["total"] ) or 0 # Allow 0.01 tolerance for floating point diff = abs(gold_total - silver_total) ok = diff < 0.01 results.append(CheckResult( "gold_correctness.revenue_total", ok, f"gold={gold_total:,.2f} silver_ref={silver_total:,.2f} diff={diff:,.2f}", ))

        # Customer KPIs: row count should equal Silver customers
        if self._table_exists(f"{self.gold}.customer_kpis") and self._table_exists(f"{self.silver}.customers"):
            gold_kpi = self._count(f"{self.gold}.customer_kpis")
            silver_cust = self._count(f"{self.silver}.customers")
            ok = gold_kpi == silver_cust
            results.append(CheckResult(
                "gold_correctness.customer_kpi_count",
                ok,
                f"gold_kpis={gold_kpi:,} silver_customers={silver_cust:,}",
            ))

        return results

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Check: Idempotent rerun
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def check_idempotency(self) -> List[CheckResult]:
        """
        Verify that Silver and Gold tables have deterministic row counts.
        This is a snapshot check — if called twice in the same session,
        counts should not change without a re-seed.
        """
        results = []
        for layer, tables in [
            (self.silver, ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]),
            (self.gold, GOLD_TABLES),
        ]:
            for table in tables:
                fqn = f"{layer}.{table}"
                if not self._table_exists(fqn):
                    continue
                # Check table has version history (Delta)
                try:
                    history = self.spark.sql(f"DESCRIBE HISTORY {fqn} LIMIT 5")
                    versions = history.count()
                    results.append(CheckResult(
                        f"idempotency.{table}.has_history",
                        versions >= 1,
                        f"Delta versions: {versions}",
                        severity="warning",
                    ))
                except Exception as e:
                    results.append(CheckResult(
                        f"idempotency.{table}.has_history",
                        False,
                        str(e),
                        severity="warning",
                    ))
        return results

layer, tables in [ (self.silver, ["customers", "invoices", "line_items", "payments", "events"]), (self.gold, GOLD_TABLES), ]: for table in tables: fqn = f"{layer}.{table}" if not self._table_exists(fqn): continue # Check table has version history (Delta) try: history = self.spark.sql(f"DESCRIBE HISTORY {fqn} LIMIT 5") versions = history.count() results.append(CheckResult( f"idempotency.{table}.has_history", versions >= 1, f"Delta versions: {versions}", severity="warning", )) except Exception as e: results.append(CheckResult( f"idempotency.{table}.has_history", False, str(e), severity="warning", )) return results

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Run all
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    def run_all(self) -> ValidationReport:
        """Run all validation checks and return a report."""
        report = ValidationReport(
            run_ts=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            catalog=self.catalog,
        )

        for check_fn in [
            self.check_row_counts,
            self.check_schemas,
            self.check_uniqueness,
            self.check_gold_correctness,
            self.check_idempotency,
        ]:
            report.checks.extend(check_fn())

        report.passed = sum(1 for c in report.checks if c.passed)
        report.failed = sum(1 for c in report.checks if not c.passed and c.severity == "error")
        report.warnings = sum(1 for c in report.checks if not c.passed and c.severity == "warning")

        return report

    @staticmethod
    def print_report(report: ValidationReport) -> None:
        """Pretty-print the validation report."""
        print(f"\n{'='*70}")
        print(f"  Medallion Validation Report — {report.run_ts}")
        print(f"  Catalog: {report.catalog}")
        print(f"{'='*70}")

        for c in report.checks:
            status = "PASS" if c.passed else ("WARN" if c.severity == "warning" else "FAIL")
            icon = {"PASS": "+", "WARN": "~", "FAIL": "!"}[status]
            print(f"  [{icon}] {status:4s} {c.check_name}")
            if not c.passed or True:
                print(f"         {c.detail}")

        print(f"\n  Summary: {report.passed} passed, {report.failed} failed, {report.warnings} warnings")
        print(f"  Result: {'ALL PASSED' if report.failed == 0 else 'FAILURES DETECTED'}")
        print(f"{'='*70}\n")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI entry point
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

if __name__ == "__main__":
    if not HAS_SPARK:
        print("PySpark not available. Run inside Databricks or a Spark-enabled environment.")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("medallion-validation").getOrCreate()
    validator = MedallionValidator(spark, catalog="dbw_ipai_dev")
    report = validator.run_all()
    validator.print_report(report)
    print(json.dumps(report.to_dict(), indent=2))

text: """Unit tests."""

"""Tests for configuration module."""

import os
from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest

from workbench.config.settings import Settings, get_settings


def test_settings_defaults():
    """Test default settings values."""
    settings = Settings()
    assert settings.env == "dev"
    assert settings.catalog == "dev_ppm"
    assert settings.schema_bronze == "bronze"
    assert settings.schema_silver == "silver"
    assert settings.schema_gold == "gold"


def test_settings_from_env():
    """Test settings loaded from environment."""
    env_vars = {
        "ENV": "prod",
        "DATABRICKS_CATALOG": "ppm",
        "SCHEMA_BRONZE": "raw",
    }
    with patch.dict(os.environ, env_vars, clear=False):
        # Clear the cache to get fresh settings
        get_settings.cache_clear()
        settings = Settings.from_env()
        assert settings.env == "prod"
        assert settings.catalog == "ppm"
        assert settings.schema_bronze == "raw"


def test_get_table_paths():
    """Test table path generation."""
    settings = Settings(catalog="test_catalog")
    assert settings.get_bronze_path("my_table") == "test_catalog.bronze.my_table"
    assert settings.get_silver_path("my_table") == "test_catalog.silver.my_table"
    assert settings.get_gold_path("my_table") == "test_catalog.gold.my_table"

# Databricks Asset Bundle: Foundation Python
# Purpose: Core Python library, medallion transforms, data quality checks, shared jobs
# Spec: spec/databricks-bundles-foundation/

bundle:
  name: foundation_python

include:
  - ../../shared/*.yml
  - resources/**/*.yml

sync:
  paths:
    - src
    - tests

variables:
  catalog:
    default: ${var.catalog}
  bronze_schema:
    default: ${var.bronze_schema}
  silver_schema:
    default: ${var.silver_schema}
  gold_schema:
    default: ${var.gold_schema}

targets:
  dev:
    default: true
    mode: development
    workspace:
      host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    variables:
      catalog: dev_ppm

  staging:
    mode: production
    workspace:
      host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    variables:
      catalog: staging_ppm

  prod:
    mode: production
    workspace:
      host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    variables:
      catalog: ppm

resources:
  jobs:
    foundation_python_smoke:
      name: "[${bundle.target}] Foundation Python Smoke"
      tasks:
        - task_key: smoke
          python_wheel_task:
            package_name: ipai_data_intelligence
            entry_point: ipai_data_intelligence.jobs.smoke:main
          new_cluster:
            spark_version: ${var.default_spark_version}
            node_type_id: ${var.default_node_type_id}
            num_workers: 0
            spark_conf:
              spark.databricks.cluster.profile: singleNode
              spark.master: "local[*]"

"""Smoke job — validates the Databricks runtime environment.

Run this job after bundle deployment to confirm:
- Python environment is functional
- Spark session is available
- Catalog and schema variables resolve correctly
"""


def main() -> None:
    """Entry point for the smoke job."""
    print("smoke: start")

    try:
        from pyspark.sql import SparkSession  # type: ignore[import-untyped]

        spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
        print(f"smoke: spark version = {spark.version}")
        print(f"smoke: catalog = {spark.catalog.currentCatalog()}")
        print(f"smoke: database = {spark.catalog.currentDatabase()}")
    except ImportError:
        print("smoke: pyspark not available (local test mode)")

    print("smoke: done")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def test_smoke() -> None:
    assert True

# Databricks Asset Bundle: Lakeflow Ingestion
# Purpose: DLT/Lakeflow ingestion pipelines for Bronze layer population
# Spec: spec/databricks-bundles-foundation/

bundle:
  name: lakeflow_ingestion

include:
  - ../../shared/*.yml
  - resources/**/*.yml

sync:
  paths:
    - src

variables:
  catalog:

  bronze_schema:

targets:
  dev:
    default: true
    mode: development
    workspace:

    variables:
      catalog: dev_ppm

  staging:
    mode: production
    workspace:
      host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    variables:
      catalog: staging_ppm

  prod:
    mode: production
    workspace:
      host: https://adb-7405610347978231.11.azuredatabricks.net
    variables:
      catalog: ppm

resources:
  jobs:
    lakeflow_ingestion_trigger:
      name: "[${bundle.target}] Lakeflow Ingestion Trigger"

      configuration:
        bundle_target: ${bundle.target}
      continuous: false

# Databricks AI/BI Dashboard: Finance PPM
# Deployed via DAB to workspace
# Reads from gold.bi_* serving views only

resources:
  dashboards:
    finance_ppm_dashboard:
      display_name: "Finance PPM Control Room"
      warehouse_id: ${var.warehouse_id}
      file_path: ../src/dashboards/finance_ppm.lvdash.json

resources:
  jobs:
    sql_warehouse_marts:
      name: "[${bundle.target}] SQL Warehouse Marts"
      tasks:
        - task_key: customer_360
          sql_task:
            warehouse_id: ${var.warehouse_id}
            file:
              path: ../../src/sql/marts/customer_360.sql

    sql_warehouse_serving:
      name: "[${bundle.target}] Finance PPM Serving Views"
      tasks:
        - task_key: deploy_serving_views
          sql_task:
            warehouse_id: ${var.warehouse_id}
            file:
              path: ../../src/sql/serving/finance_ppm_serving.sql

        - task_key: deploy_kpi_queries
          depends_on:
            - task_key: deploy_serving_views
          sql_task:
            warehouse_id: ${var.warehouse_id}
            file:
              path: ../../src/sql/serving/finance_ppm_kpis.sql

-- =============================================================================
-- Serving Schema Setup
-- Ensures the gold schema exists and has appropriate comments.
-- Run once per environment via SQL Warehouse.
-- =============================================================================

-- The serving views live in gold schema alongside the gold tables.
-- This is the approved pattern per docs/architecture/POWER_BI_ON_GOLD.md:
--   bi_* prefix identifies BI-serving objects within gold.

-- Verify catalog and schema exist
CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS ${catalog}.gold
COMMENT 'Gold layer: business-ready aggregates and BI serving views';

-- Tag serving views with properties for discoverability
-- (Applied individually in the serving SQL; this script ensures the schema exists)

=============================================================================
-- Finance PPM KPI Queries
-- Purpose: Named saved queries for Databricks SQL dashboards.
--          Each query is a standalone KPI suitable for a dashboard tile.
-- SQL Warehouse: e7d89eabce4c330c
-- =============================================================================

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 1: Revenue by Period
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_revenue_by_period
SELECT
    fiscal_year,
    fiscal_month,
    fiscal_quarter,
    COUNT(*)              AS entry_count,
    SUM(amount_total)     AS total_revenue,
    SUM(amount_untaxed)   AS revenue_before_tax,
    SUM(amount_tax)       AS total_tax
FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_account_moves
WHERE move_type IN ('out_invoice', 'out_refund')
  AND state = 'posted'
GROUP BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month, fiscal_quarter
ORDER BY fiscal_year DESC, fiscal_month DESC;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 2: AP/AR Cash Position
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_ap_ar_position
SELECT * FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_ap_ar_summary;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 3: Client Revenue Ranking
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_client_revenue_ranking
SELECT *
FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_client_revenue
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 4: Project Task Completion
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_project_task_completion
SELECT
    p.name                AS project_name,
    COUNT(*)              AS total_tasks,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t.is_completed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed_tasks,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t.is_overdue THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)   AS overdue_tasks,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN t.is_completed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1)
                          AS completion_pct
FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_project_tasks t
LEFT JOIN ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_project p ON t.project_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY total_tasks DESC;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 5: Expense Liquidation Health
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_expense_health
SELECT * FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_expense_health;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 6: Monthly Revenue Trend
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

END) AS overdue_tasks, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN t.is_completed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS completion_pct FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_project_tasks t LEFT JOIN ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_project p ON t.project_id = p.id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_tasks DESC; -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- KPI 5: Expense Liquidation Health -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- @name: kpi_expense_health SELECT * FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_expense_health; -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- KPI 6: Monthly Revenue Trend -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- @name: kpi_monthly_revenue_trend
SELECT
    fiscal_year,
    fiscal_month,
    SUM(amount_total) AS monthly_revenue,
    LAG(SUM(amount_total)) OVER (ORDER BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month) AS prev_month_revenue,
    ROUND(100.0 * (SUM(amount_total) - LAG(SUM(amount_total)) OVER (ORDER BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month))
        / NULLIF(LAG(SUM(amount_total)) OVER (ORDER BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month), 0), 1)
        AS mom_growth_pct
FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_account_moves
WHERE move_type IN ('out_invoice', 'out_refund')
  AND state = 'posted'
GROUP BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month
ORDER BY fiscal_year, fiscal_month;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- KPI 7: Task Stage Distribution
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- @name: kpi_task_stage_distribution
SELECT
    s.name                AS stage_name,
    COUNT(*)              AS task_count
FROM ${catalog}.gold.bi_project_tasks t
LEFT JOIN ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_task_stage s ON t.stage_id = s.id
GROUP BY s.name
ORDER BY task_count DESC;

=============================================================================
-- Finance PPM Serving Layer
-- Purpose: Stable BI-facing views over gold tables. These are the ONLY objects
--          that Power BI semantic models and Databricks dashboards should read.
-- Grain, measures, and dimensions are declared per view.
-- Owner: data-intelligence repo
-- SQL Warehouse: e7d89eabce4c330c
-- =============================================================================

-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 1. bi_account_moves — Financial transaction fact
-- Grain: one accounting journal entry (account.move)
-- Measures: amount_total, debit_total, credit_total, entry_count
-- Dimensions: dim_project, dim_analytic_account
-- Refresh: daily (DLT pipeline)
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_account_moves AS
SELECT
    id                    AS move_id,
    name                  AS entry_number,
    date                  AS posting_date,
    move_type,
    state,
    partner_id,
    journal_id,
    company_id,
    amount_total,
    amount_residual,
    amount_untaxed,
    amount_tax,
    currency_id,
    YEAR(date)            AS fiscal_year,
    MONTH(date)           AS fiscal_month,
    QUARTER(date)         AS fiscal_quarter,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.fact_account_move
WHERE state IN ('posted', 'draft');


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 2. bi_project_tasks — Project execution fact
-- Grain: one project task
-- Measures: task_count, completed_count, overdue_count
-- Dimensions: dim_project, dim_task_stage, dim_tag
-- Refresh: daily
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_project_tasks AS
SELECT
    id                    AS task_id,
    name                  AS task_name,
    project_id,
    stage_id,
    user_ids              AS assigned_user_ids,
    date_deadline,
    date_last_stage_update,
    priority,
    kanban_state,
    CASE
        WHEN date_deadline < CURRENT_DATE() AND kanban_state != 'done'
        THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE
    END                   AS is_overdue,
    CASE
        WHEN kanban_state = 'done' THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE
    END                   AS is_completed,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.fact_project_task;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 3. bi_client_revenue — Revenue by client
-- Grain: one client
-- Measures: total_revenue, invoice_count
-- Dimensions: (standalone — client is the dimension)
-- Refresh: daily
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_client_revenue AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.client_revenue;

FALSE END AS is_overdue, CASE WHEN kanban_state = 'done' THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_completed, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS _serving_refreshed_at FROM ${catalog}.gold.fact_project_task; -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- 3. bi_client_revenue — Revenue by client -- Grain: one client -- Measures: total_revenue, invoice_count -- Dimensions: (standalone — client is the dimension) -- Refresh: daily -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_client_revenue AS SELECT *, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS _serving_refreshed_at FROM ${catalog}.gold.client_revenue;-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 4. bi_ap_ar_summary — Accounts payable/receivable cash position
-- Grain: aggregate snapshot
-- Measures: total_receivable, total_payable, net_position
-- Dimensions: none (single-row summary)
-- Refresh: daily
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_ap_ar_summary AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.ap_ar_cash_summary;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 5. bi_expense_health — Expense liquidation health
-- Grain: aggregate snapshot
-- Measures: liquidation_rate, pending_amount, approved_amount
-- Dimensions: none
-- Refresh: daily
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_expense_health AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.expense_liquidation_health;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 6. bi_dim_project — Project dimension
-- Grain: one project
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_project AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_project;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 7. bi_dim_analytic_account — Cost center / analytic dimension
-- Grain: one analytic account
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_analytic_account AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_analytic_account;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 8. bi_dim_task_stage — Task stage dimension
-- Grain: one task stage
-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_task_stage AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_task_stage;


-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- 9. bi_dim_tag — Tag dimension
-- Grain: one tag

/ analytic dimension -- Grain: one analytic account -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_analytic_account AS SELECT *, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS _serving_refreshed_at FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_analytic_account; -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- 8. bi_dim_task_stage — Task stage dimension -- Grain: one task stage -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_task_stage AS SELECT *, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP() AS _serving_refreshed_at FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_task_stage; -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- -- 9. bi_dim_tag — Tag dimension -- Grain: one tag-- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${catalog}.gold.bi_dim_tag AS
SELECT
    *,
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP()   AS _serving_refreshed_at
FROM ${catalog}.gold.dim_tag;

# Shared variables consumed by all Databricks bundles.
# Each bundle includes this file via: include: [../../shared/*.yml]
# Do not put job or pipeline definitions here.

variables:
  workspace_host:
    description: Databricks workspace URL
    default: ""

  catalog:
    description: Unity Catalog name
    default: "ipai_dev"

  bronze_schema:
    description: Bronze layer schema name
    default: "bronze"

  silver_schema:
    description: Silver layer schema name
    default: "silver"

  gold_schema:
    description: Gold layer schema name
    default: "gold"

  warehouse_id:
    description: SQL Warehouse ID for SQL tasks
    default: ""

  default_node_type_id:
    description: Default compute node type
    default: "Standard_DS3_v2"

  default_spark_version:
    description: Default Databricks Runtime version
    default: "15.4.x-scala2.12"

metadata.title: Shared variables consumed by all Databricks bundles.
